subject: You're Invited: Save The Date for Our 8th Annual CS Education Heroes Award Ceremony!
body:
Hello,

We’re excited to announce 9 Dots' Save the Date for this year's 8th Annual
CS Education Heroes event! 🏅🎉

Teachers are the cornerstone of our classrooms, and it’s a true privilege

subject: We Received Your 2026–27 FAFSA Form
body:
Processing takes one to three business days. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Click here to view this email as a web page.
https://rttrack.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=t5f09ff7e,f7c2319,20eb530 We Received Your FAFSA ® Form Cristina Le, We received your 2026–27 Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA ® ) form. We'll process your form in one to three business days and notify you when it's done. You can also track your status on your " https://rttrack.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=t5f09ff7e,f7c2319,20eb531 My Activity " page.  

Preview of Your Results

This is a preview of your results. The exact amounts may change once your FAFSA® form has finished processing.

Estimated Pell Grant Eligibility
Based on the eligibility criteria, you may be eligible for a Federal Pell Grant of up to $1,550. You may also be eligible for other federal, state, or institutional grants; scholarships; and/or work-study programs.

Estimated Federal Aid Eligibility
Your student aid eligibility will be calculated by your college or career school based on your FAFSA form information.

 

What Happens Next

Receive Notification of Processing
We will process your FAFSA® form within one to three days. You can view the status of your FAFSA form online.
 
Watch for School Communications
Your selected schools will receive your FAFSA® information and will reach out to you if they need additional details. They will also contact you with your financial aid offers. Once received, you can contact the schools' financial aid offices directly to ask questions about the offers.
 
Start Your Application for California State-Based Aid

Apply for state aid.
Submission Information You may need this information if you talk to the financial aid administrator at your college or career school. Date Received: April 15, 2026 Data Release Number (DRN): 9350 You may need to provide your DRN to your school's financial aid administrator if you want them to make changes to your FAFSA form on your behalf. Treat your DRN as private information like you would the PIN number for your debit card. Estimated Student Aid Index (SAI) Schools use your SAI to determine your federal https://rttrack.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=t5f09ff7e,f7c2319,20eb532 student aid eligibility . The SAI is not a measure of how much student aid you'll receive or how much you'll pay for college. Estimated Student Aid Index (SAI) = 5847 Related Resources 1 https://rttrack.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=t5f09ff7e,f7c2319,20eb533 What happens after I submit my FAFSA ® form? 2 https://rttrack.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=t5f09ff7e,f7c2319,20eb534 When should I correct my FAFSA ® form? 3 https://rttrack.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=t5f09ff7e,f7c2319,20eb535 How do I correct my FAFSA ® form?  

Need help filling out the FAFSA® form or have questions about your form? Log in to StudentAid.gov to see your form's status, and check out the FAFSA® Help Center for application tips and other useful information.

Sign up for text alerts for important updates on your FAFSA form.

This email was sent by: Office of Federal Student Aid
U.S. Department of Education
400 Maryland Ave SW,
Washington, DC 20002

 

Please do not reply to this email. Messages sent to this email address are not monitored. If you wish to contact us, please use the StudentAid.gov contact page. For more information about financial aid, visit StudentAid.gov.

Cristina, get up to 40% off your next trip this week. Your promo has automatically been applied to your account and is valid through April 20, 2026 12:00 AM.
Take a trip
 

What’s Uber got for you?

Wherever you’re going, whatever you’re doing, Uber has something for everyone.

Request on-demand rides.

Door-to-door pickup and dropoff.

Seamless payments in-app, so you don’t need your wallet.

Ability to track and share trip info with family and friends.
Explore more
Sign up for an Uber One membership to start saving!
Enjoy 6% Uber One credits on eligible rides, $0 Delivery Fee on eligible orders, and more. Members save $30 per month on average.
Get 4 weeks free
 
 
 
Maximum savings of $10 per ride. Valid for up to one ride. The promotional savings of a ride can vary by route and time of day. Offer is valid in United States only and expires at April 20, 2026 12:00 AM. Valid only on select rides based on your location. See in-app for full list. Offer is applicable only for rides taken on a personal profile. Promotion does not apply to surcharges, government fees, tolls, or tips and cannot be combined with other offers or savings. For accounts with multiple valid promo codes, the promo with the highest savings will automatically apply to a rider’s next trip. Offer is non-transferable. Offer and terms are subject to change. Offer is not applicable on trips booked with Uber Reserve.
 
 
 
Benefits available only where Uber operates for eligible stores and rides marked with the Uber One icon. Other fees & taxes may apply depending on what market you’re in, but do not count towards order minimums or Uber One credits benefits. Minimum order may be required to receive waived Delivery Fee on standard delivery, and discounted Service Fee, from participating stores (if applicable in your country). Additional and optional fees may be charged for Priority Delivery and Long Range Delivery (if applicable in your country). Where applicable, Uber One credits are earned after completion of eligible rides and expire on the dates stated in the Wallet section of your Uber app. Subject to change. Additional detail on Uber One benefits in your country are available here. Amount you could save based on average savings of members in your country from promos and member pricing.

View the Renewable Membership T&Cs.

Uber One is a subscription that auto-renews (as applicable for your market). Your payment method will be charged $9.99/month or $96/year, depending on the plan you select, upon the conclusion of any applicable free trial or promotional period. In order to ensure continuation of your subscription, you agree we may charge you up to one day before your auto-renewal date. If you cancel close to your renewal date, you may see a charge for the next cycle. This charge will be automatically refunded as long as you canceled before the renewal date. Refunds may take a few days to appear, depending on your card provider. If you don’t receive it, please contact support. Automatic refund not yet available in all Uber One markets.

Hi Lauren,

On the topic of my laptop, I've been having some trouble with it rebooting unexpectedly from time to time. I had my touch base with Tina this week, and we were wondering how my badge and laptop assignment would work since I am not attending orientation? I still have my badge from last summer at the AD building in Peoria, but I believe the access is different for each office.

and laptop assignment would work since I am not attending orientation? I
still have my badge from last summer at the AD building in Peoria, but I
believe the access is different for each office.

Additionally, would I still need to complete the I-9 verification even
though I am working with my current team in IT up until my next start date?

Thank you so much in advance and I look forward to hearing from you soon!


Warm regards,
Cristina Le

subject: Kickstart a Career That Makes a Difference! 🌍
body:
April 2026 

 Early Careers That 
  Make a Difference 🌍  

 Hi Cristina, 

	Starting your career is more than landing a job. It is about finding purpose, growth, and a place where your work matters. This month, hear honest advice from IBMers reflecting on their younger selves, explore how graduates build meaningful careers from day one, and see how a design intern turned inspiration into impact. 

	Plus, level up your AI skills with hands-on learning, discover a new way of building software with Agentic AI and learn how to protect yourself from fake job offers. Your future starts now.   
   [1] 

	Are you confused about the authenticity of your job offer or suspicious about the recruitment communication you are receiving? Recruitment scams are on the rise, so it’s important to stay vigilant and protect your personal information. 
 [2] Learn more about recruitment scams here 

Can’t miss this 👇?

   [3]    

	From curious girls to women shaping what’s next, IBMers share the advice they would give their younger selves. 

	[4] Read it on Instagram   	  [5]    

	Discover why graduates choose IBM for careers that combine real responsibility, modern tech, and impact from day one. 

	[6] Explore the blog   	  [7]    

	Inspired by IBM design legends, Ashish joined as an intern and learned that great design is about people, not just pixels. 

	[8] Read it on Linkedin   

 Your free monthly brain booster ? 

 Note: You'll have to sign into our free SkillsBuild learning platform to access the course below. 

 [9]  Artificial Intelligence Skills Accelerator   

	 Ready to build real AI skills? Join the IBM SkillsBuild AI Skills Accelerator. Learn machine learning, NLP and Generative AI through hands-on labs and earn 6 digital badges. Spots are limited!   

  	 [10]     	   

 Around the world 🌍

	What Is Vibe Coding? Building Software with Agentic AI 

	Can you code by following the vibes? Cedric Clyburn explains vibe coding, where Agentic AI and prompt engineering combine to streamline AI-assisted development. [11]  Learn more about vibe coding   

Update Your Profile!

	Been working for 2+ years? You're no longer Entry Level! 

	Update your profile to get the Professionals Newsletter with content for your career stage. 

	1. Log into your [12] IBM Account.  

	2. Go to the "Your Information" tab and click "Edit your information".  

	3. Select Professional (>3 years) in the "Experience Level" field.  

	4. Save your changes! 

  	 [13]     	   

 📢 Stay ahead: set up job alerts 

	Be the first to know when new roles open that match your skills and interests. 

	 Here’s how to set up your personalized alerts:  

	1. Log in to your [14] IBM account. 

	2. Go to the "Job Alerts" tab and click "Create Job Alert." 

	3. Fill out the form by entering relevant keywords and selecting other preferences to tailor alerts to your interests. 

	4. Click "Submit", and you’re all set! 

	You'll start receiving job alerts to your email based on your chosen frequency.  

 This is the sign you've been looking for!

 [15]  Apply to our open roles   	   

	Keep in touch! 

	The IBM Talent Acquisition Team  

	Did a friend forward this to you? 

	 You can [16] subscribe here.    

	 Connect with us 
 [17]  [18]  [19]  [20]    

 ©IBM Corporation 2026. IBM, the IBM logo, IBM Cloud™ and ibm.com are trademarks of the International Business Machines Corp., registered in many jurisdictions worldwide. This document is current as of the initial date of publication and may be changed by IBM at any time. Not all offerings are available in every country in which IBM operates.

 To ensure delivery to your inbox, please add talent@ibm.com to your address book. Please do not reply to this email, as we are unable to respond from this address.

 If you’d like us to refrain from sending you similar e-mails in the future, please click on the unsubscribe link below. You may also mail a written request to IBM US at: IBM Corporation, 11501 Burnet Rd, Bldg 904 3B-000, Austin, TX 78758.   

This email is being sent on behalf of IBM. To unsubscribe from further emails on this list, please click [21] here.

subject: Caterpillar Intern Newsletter : April 2026
body:
[cid:image001.png@01DCC806.C6646720]


WHAT DO YOU WANT TO GET OUT OF YOUR INTERNSHIP?

As a Caterpillar intern, you will be a part of meaningful work that impacts lives across the globe starting from day one. Gaining real-world work experience is an essential part of starting your career. However, the learning doesn’t stop there: we provide helpful training, reliable mentors, growth opportunities, and more.

It is our goal that your internship at Caterpillar will provide you with first-hand experience in the working world, allow you to learn more about your capabilities, explore your strengths, and better understand your career trajectory.
As you prepare to join #TeamCaterpillar, we encourage you to think about what you want to get out of your internship. Here are some of the key milestones coming your way as you approach your start date!


[cid:image002.png@01DCC806.C6646720]

RELOCATION REMINDERS
Caterpillar offers relocation benefits to help offset housing costs and support smart financial decisions. Check the attached relocation roadmap for an overview of the process and tips to get started.
Our relocation partner, Cartus, will contact you after you’ve completed your pre-employment steps (background check, drug screen, etc.). They are reaching out to interns by start date, so thank you for your patience.
Get a Head Start. You don’t need to wait for Cartus to begin your search:

  *   Research early: Compare cost, availability, location, amenities and short-term housing options. Cartus recommendations are a starting point—not a full list.
  *   Secure housing ASAP: Availability is limited, and prices may increase closer to your start date. Your offer letter can serve as proof of employment if needed.
Connect with your Cartus consultant if you have questions or run into housing availability issues.

CORPORATE INTERN ORIENTATION PROGRAM
The 2026 Corporate Intern Orientation will take place on Tuesday, May 19 at the Edwards Demonstration & Learning Center. Be prepared to experience Caterpillar in an environment unlike any other! Here are a few important items to note for those who will be joining us onsite. More information will be shared closer to the program!

  *   We have booked a hotel for any interns outside of the Peoria Area (Embassy Suites by Hilton East Peoria Riverfront Hotel and Conference Center).
  *   If you haven’t done so already, please reach out to Lauren Southwood if you plan to take the provided bus from Chicago to Peoria and back for intern orientation.
  *   Pants and close-toed shoes are required for orientation (think jeans with no rips/holes/tears and sneakers/boots).
  *   Caterpillar will provide transportation (bus) to and from your hotel and/or short-term housing to the Edwards Demonstration & Learning Center. A schedule will be provided in the coming weeks.

Your manager will be reaching out soon to schedule a pre-internship touch base. If you have not heard from your manager by 5/1 please let Lauren Southwood know.

SUBMIT A SELFIE VIDEO TODAY!
Be featured in a video during Intern Orientation! Whether you’re a first‑time intern or a returning intern, we’d love a short 10–15 second vertical selfie video from you.
New interns: Answer the question: Why did you choose Caterpillar for your internship? [Sample: Hi, I’m [Name] and I’ll be working in [Role]. I picked Caterpillar for my internship because…]
Returning interns: Answer the question: What is one word or piece of advice you would give to incoming interns? [Sample: Hi, I’m [Name] and one piece of advice I’d give to incoming interns is…]
Start with your name and role/business area, record in a quiet space, and make sure no logos are present unless they’re Caterpillar related
Submit by April 25 to universityrelations@cat.com<mailto:universityrelations@cat.com>
Subject Line (as appropriate)

  *   Name – Function – New Intern Selfie Video
  *   Name – Function – Returning Intern Selfie Video
Thank you if you already submitted your video! We can’t wait to feature you!

IMPORTANT: EMPLOYMENT DOCUMENTS CHECKLIST
As part of your pre-onboarding process, you’ll need to complete Form I-9 (Employment Eligibility Verification). This is a legal requirement for employers to confirm the identity and work authorization of all new employees hired in the United States. We use an automated I-9 verification process through our partner, First Advantage. Watch your inbox (and spam folder) for an email from “First Advantage” with instructions and next steps. Please review the attached checklist (A-Notice to Prospective Applicants form) to ensure you have the required documents to complete your I-9 verification. Having everything prepared ahead of time will help avoid delays or issues with your start date.

GROUP CHAT FOR ALL INTERNS
Join our GroupMe chat to connect with interns across Caterpillar—meet new people, find roommates or carpool partners, and connect over shared interests. It’s an easy way to build community beyond your business unit or function. Review the attached GroupMe Rules of Engagement and JOIN HERE!<https://groupme.com/join_group/112859477/tPqV79BQ>
Note: Participation is completely optional.

ACTION ITEMS
If you haven’t already, please complete the following:

  *   Update your personal information in Workday (new employees only), using the instructions sent by Workday.
  *   Send your unoffical transcripts by April 1, including completed Fall 2025 courses and in-progress Spring 2026 courses, with your cumulative GPA.
     *   Please send transcripts to Lauren Southwood

STAY UP TO DATE ON ALL THINGS CATERPILLAR
Caterpillar Elevates Skilled Trades Through Global Technician and Operator Challenges<https://www.caterpillar.com/en/news/corporate-press-releases/h/gtgoc-operator-challenges.html>

[cid:image003.png@01DCC806.C6646720]

We can’t wait to see the work you will do to help build a better world!

Stay connected with us! Tag us using #iAmCaterpillar
Facebook<https://www.facebook.com/catcareers/> | LinkedIn<https://www.linkedin.com/company/caterpillar-inc/> | X<https://twitter.com/CaterpillarJobs> | Instagram<https://www.instagram.com/wearecaterpillar/>



Thank you,

Lauren Southwood
Digital Talent Acquisition Partner
Caterpillar Inc. | Digital, Technology, and Distribution
Office: 309.578.6574 | Mobile: 309.357.9155 | Email: lauren.southwood@cat.com<mailto:lauren.southwood@cat.com>
StrengthsFinder34 Signature Themes
Relator | Command | Achiever | Futuristic | Adaptability
Connect with me on LinkedIn<https://www.linkedin.com/in/laurensouthwood/>

**My work hours may not be yours. Please do not feel obligated to respond outside of your normal work hours**




Caterpillar: Confidential Green

* TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Earn up to 100,000 Bonus Points: You can receive 15,000 bonus points for each friend you refer through this Refer-A-Friend offer (up to 7), who is approved for the card. Maximum bonus points accumulation for this offer is 100,000 bonus points. If 10 or more of your friends use the referral URL within a month to apply, at Chase’s discretion you may not receive any bonus points for the friends who apply that month and are approved for the card. We may provide you with future opportunities to earn bonus points by referring your friends to any participating Chase Sapphire Credit Card. Participating Chase Sapphire Cards include: Chase Sapphire Preferred and Chase Sapphire Reserve. You may earn bonus points up to the stated maximum for each offer, however, please note that if you take advantage of multiple opportunities within one calendar year that the maximum number of bonus points that can be awarded from all referral offers is 100,000 per calendar year. In the event the bonus points you earn from all referral offers exceeds the 100,000 per calendar year limit, you may be awarded a partial amount to keep your total bonus at 100,000 bonus points. For example if you have already accumulated 90,000 bonus points from refer-a-friend offers that calendar year and the maximum is 100,000 bonus points per calendar year, then the next award will be 10,000 even if the offer states 15,000. The value of this offer or program may result in miscellaneous income received from Chase and Chase may be required to send you, and file with the IRS, a Form 1099-MISC (Miscellaneous Information) or Form 1042-S (Foreign Person’s U.S. Source Income Subject to Withholding) for the year in which you participate and are awarded the benefits of the offer/program. You are responsible for any tax liability related to participating in the offer/program. Please consult your own tax advisor if you have any questions about how this could affect your taxes. This offer is subject to change and may be cancelled at any time without notice. To be eligible for this bonus offer, account must be open and not in default at the time of fulfillment. Please allow up to 8 weeks after each new cardmember’s approval for bonus points to post to your account. Your bonus will appear on your statement as “Refer-A-Friend bonus.” You are not eligible for a referral bonus if you use your own referral URL to apply for a different card. Due to the confidential nature of the credit approval process, we cannot disclose information on who has applied, nor will you be notified whether we approve or decline any application(s) from the individual(s) you refer. Otherwise a $15 Monthly Service Fee will apply. Qualifying electronic deposits include payments from your employer or government entities (benefits, tax refunds, etc.), made using the ACH network, Real Time Payment (RTP(R)), FedNow(R) Service, or third-party services that facilitate payments to your debit card using the Visa(R) or
 Mastercard(R) network. Transactions such as Zelle(R), cash, checks, wire transfers, and interest payments do not count as qualifying electronic deposits. Qualifying personal deposits include balances in this account and any Chase First CheckingSM, Chase SavingsSM, CDs, as well as certain Chase Retirement CDs or Chase Retirement Money Market accounts. Qualifying investments include balances in certain investment and annuity products offered through JPMorgan Chase & Co. and its affiliates and agencies. We use daily balances for most investment products. Some third-party providers report balances on a periodic basis and we will use the most current balance reported to calculate the average beginning day balance. Balances in 529 plans, donor-advised funds, and certain retirement plan
 investment accounts do not qualify. Qualifying linked checking accounts include J.P. Morgan Private Client Checking Plus, J.P. Morgan Classic Checking, Chase Private Client CheckingSM, Chase Sapphire(R) Checking, Chase Premier Plus CheckingSM, Chase Performance Business Checking(R), or Chase Performance Business Checking With Interest(R). For personal accounts, there must be a common owner. For business accounts, the owner of the linked personal account must be a direct owner of the business and signer on the business account.

Chase SavingsSM: $0 Monthly Service Fee when you have any one of the following during each statement period: Option #1: $300+ balance at the beginning of each day; or, Option #2: $25+ in total Autosave or other repeating automatic transfers from your personal Chase checking account (available only through chase.com 
 or Chase Mobile(R)); or, Option #3: Link this account to a qualifying checking account; or,Option #4: Link this account to a Chase College CheckingSM account that is enrolled in Overdraft Protection; or, Option #5: An account owner who is under the age of 25. Otherwise a $5 Monthly Service Fee will apply. Qualifying linked checking accounts include J.P. Morgan Private Client Checking Plus, J.P. Morgan Classic Checking, Chase Private Client CheckingSM, Chase Sapphire(R) Checking or Chase Premier Plus CheckingSM. For personal accounts, there must be a common owner.

Both offers are not available to those whose accounts have been closed within 90 days or closed with a negative balance within the last 3 years. You can receive only one new checking and one new savings account opening related bonus every two years from the last coupon enrollment date and only one bonus per account. Coupon is good for one-time use. To receive the checking bonus: 1) Open a new Chase Total Checking account, which is subject to approval; and 2) Have your direct deposit made into the new checking account within 90 days of coupon enrollment. Your direct deposit needs to be an electronic deposit of your paycheck,
 pension or government benefits (such as Social Security) from your employer or the government. Person to Person payments (such as Zelle(R)) are not considered a direct deposit. Micro-deposits do not qualify as a direct deposit for the bonus. Micro-deposits are small deposits, typically less than $1, that are sent to your account to verify it is the correct account. After you have completed all the above checking requirements, we'll deposit the bonus in your new account within 15 days. To receive the savings bonus: 1) Open a new Chase SavingsSM account, which is subject to approval; 2) Deposit a total of $15,000 or more in new money into the new savings account within 30 days of coupon enrollment. The new money cannot be funds held by you at Chase or its affiliates; and 3) Maintain at
 least a $15,000 balance in the new savings account for 90 days from the coupon enrollment. After you have completed all the above savings requirements, we'll deposit the bonus in your new account within 15 days. To receive the extra bonus: You must open the checking and savings account at the same time and complete all requirements above for both the checking bonus and savings bonus. After you have completed all requirements, we will deposit the remaining bonus due in your new account within 15 days. To receive any of the above bonuses, the enrolled account must not be closed or restricted at the time of payout. Eligibility may be limited based on account ownership. The Annual Percentage Yield (APY) for Chase SavingsSM, effective as of 03/27/2026

subject: New Post Available | Check Out The Latest News
sender: Women in Tech Mentorship New Post <hello@oneuponedown.org>
body:
| ## From Execution to Strategic Mindset Masterclass 2: Self-Awareness & Emotional Regulation

📅 Thursday, 9 April · 6pm CET

How you show up under pressure is your leadership brand.

The higher you go, the more your reactions, patterns, and presence shape how others experience you - and how much they trust you. In this session, Michal Lasman, global go-to-market and product marketing leader, executive & leadership coach, will guide us through the inner work of strategic leadership: understanding our triggers, building self-awareness, and learning to respond with intention rather than react from habit.

This masterclass provides practical leadership development for people who want to lead with clarity when it matters most.

You'll leave with:
→ A sharper understanding of how your behaviour shapes trust
→ Tools to self-regulate in high-pressure moments
→ Greater awareness of the patterns that help and hinder your leadership

This is for you if you want to lead with more consistency, composure, and credibility.

body:

Tina Williams
Software Engineering Manager
Telematics DataHub | Cat Digital | Caterpillar Inc.
Phone: (309)368-0741 (C)
Email: Williams_Tina_M@cat.com<mailto:Williams_Tina_M@cat.com>

[Image][Image]



Caterpillar: Confidential Green

From: Cristina Le <cristinathoale@gmail.com>
Date: Monday, April 6, 2026 at 2:25 PM
To: Tina Williams <Williams_Tina_M@cat.com>
Subject: IDE Preference

Be Careful: Email is from an External Source
Use caution when opening emails and do not click on links or attachments from unknown senders. For more resources, visit security.cat.com/phishing.

Hi Tina,

I realized that there was one question I forgot to ask during our meeting. What IDE does the team typically use? I know IntelliJ is a popular choice for Java developers, since I'm most familiar with VS Code, I wanted to check if I might need to get comfortable with a different IDE.

Thanks so much, and I hope you have a great week!

Warm Regards,
Cristina Le

subject: IDE Preference
body:
Hi Tina,

I realized that there was one question I forgot to ask during our meeting.
What IDE does the team typically use? I know IntelliJ is a popular choice
for Java developers, since I'm most familiar with VS Code, I wanted to
check if I might need to get comfortable with a different IDE.

Thanks so much, and I hope you have a great week!

Warm Regards,
Cristina Le

subject: Guest Stay Request
body:
Hello, 

I’m excited to be moving into the Arc for the summer and wanted to check if 
it would be okay for my boyfriend to stay as a guest for one week, starting 
the day after I move in. I'll be coming to Chicago from California and was 
hoping to have him there to help me move into my studio, since he is from 
Indianapolis and more familiar with the city. 

We’ll both be working throughout the week and will be sure not to interfere 
with the tranquility of other tenants. I noticed in the lease that guests 
staying more than two nights require approval, and I don’t want to overstep.

Please let me know if this is possible or if there are any specific 
requirements I should be aware of. Thank you so much for your time!


Best,
Cristina Le

subject: Ready. Set. GO!
body:
Discover something new this spring with CityPASS® tickets.

Spring Brings New Additions to CityPASS® Programs

We're happy to welcome three premier arts and entertainment attractions to CityPASS programs in Boston, Dallas and Toronto.   


Museum of Fine Arts, Boston

MFA, Boston's multifaceted collection of nearly half a million works spans ancient to contemporary art. More than 100 galleries tell a comprehensive story of human history.


Go to find yourself in the narrative.

AT&T Stadium Tours

Experience the enduring magnetism of the Dallas Cowboys and the thrill of AT&T Stadium, the largest domed structure in the world (the Statue of Liberty could fit inside!). 


Go to learn all the ways AT&T Stadium reinforces the Texas reputation for going big.

Art Gallery of Ontario

Explore the Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO), one of North America’s largest art museums with more than 120,000 works. AGO presents wide-ranging exhibitions, including acquisitions from diverse and underrepresented artists from around the world. 


Go to see where your interests might lead you.

CityPASS tickets take you to the best attractions in 17 North American destinations.


You have one year from the date of purchase to start using your CityPASS tickets. Once activated, tickets are valid for a 9-day period (7 days in Denver) starting with your first attraction visit or reservation date. (Validity for theme park tickets in Orlando and Southern California varies.)

Just GO!

30 Million Travelers Since 1997

subject: Monday: Sam Altman Live on the Future of AI
body:
Altman, NASCAR, Newsrooms, and Biological Intelligence in the OpenAI Forum this April.

Dear Cristina,

We’re racing into April with playoff energy.

Monday, Sam Altman returns to the Forum  (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37j7hjrlW5BWSxg6lZ3nwW8XKmW33k-k6tW2KqgWQ96jf6rW1f2Wmh7d0X1PW7qZ95n2v2N0lW6_03Xb26TCSWW2FyV9C37ZpV2W7D248k6gtP3dW3xlWJJ8HlLRDW8Hd-yB435r9pW6XMbM84MMXFBW4HdXPf7jBPh9W7PJ4KX33yGnkN54p8ZSpWVhfVTBPby62zCt-W3Slt513h6VyjW2w1hLM4R6x9JW5QPWZ760ZlylN1VvTCnFQZCmW2tNvSv41fmtjW2RjKjG3YwJBxW2Tq2cb2HP0r6W2ZyR8j2HBrvvW4khkqF2p-JY6W13fjzk80NrzFW5fvtW97bKYxJW330-Z31Q59wjW5R1LHr1NS9TgW50g61S6PQmFPW92D-sR8bgXDDN45cHFzj6nByW6LcJpw7vDtMbW6cx48d3cGMWqW7Cb-Hn8Hv4fhW442Wx-35sLM0W58SwPJ7KFBs6W4-3jzH1lV97fN3BMKb1NP15zW3Jcp_84zTgLtN3Jzlq_lcjKlW84-NH_1HDt5-W4cv1Q71w1SlZW2CBTSX7NbP-fW5jxx5N6xv66JV67nT67bz30YW1v1gsm6MbPm9W7SvhfF3-VXSbW3Shy061DCMpKW486Scv8K3jxPW1Xg4GD4b-D4vVl5CVz17dJkKf1xx7v804 ) with our research leadership to discuss how OpenAI is building the next phase of AI. Forum members are invited to this exclusive opportunity to join the conversation and ask questions live.

But first—tomorrow, we’ll host journalists, editors, and technologists to sharehow AI is reshaping newsrooms (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37W7hjrlW6N2kFb6lZ3lCW22G5wN6Cp2WvW8_3K1_79b-GZVfywHz9jMnH5W3xBvJV8Zxn01N2b655gYvSYxW1VcGMB1C_FQWW4Y0ryq2LZhczW3ZjW2D2qB9GFN1mdcXGxK71jW8JzWfb8s7ZnVVC97rC6hzQSBW48KQJJ2KZDQ5W2fVV1F1n_yCKW3z6btZ6z64kJW7x5RzP46VzM1W1wjlHR88r4J1W36CTdS1K9nnvW4mVg6M9d2ztfW8psd7L3wmr7hN86sX0GkQSy7VZnGwZ4gsB1RW51Wzbc8sjtmwW2B7VBR5yKY_rW6KHRsp3r1H51VM2cnr3J-lqDW2NsKpC2H27LrVB93ld7ZBg6_W8JkrdW6khf5TW8lGGCt79_TfMW2Gt12-38nynJW1KGN3T7pB8SrW7H0xD488f1yZW50ZzZ-8QFrygN5jp5zQz_9DLW60c9Xd5pgVRvW6rXdmr3M2sfhV5xQKD4t2-SqW6ythZW3sWgC8W70Q-gF33T06NN7cC_t1J2P1YW87Dl_D9chxjrN2hK3XPynynGW23rpty64tSxKN3wHfNsqWYjlW6QPKC73MpYSzW1hpzWf54CwfzVpnVXH7zm6rZW2dnZM-6KPC1zW70Yj0W3_gsd2N83DfkM5sVjpW3Y8mNH5YlLNYW6cPSGB4H8QYhW4Vc6h85WR98XW1V2_-K5zh_KMdvPMZg04 ) —what responsible use looks like, and what it means for the future of journalism. Live Q&A and demos to follow.

We’re big sports fans in the Forum. It’s not just about winning—it’s how legendary teams build community, expand access, and innovate on and off the court, field, track, and rink. The most enduring teams succeed by investing in people, creating shared purpose, and showing up for their communities. The same is increasingly true of how we build and use AI.

For this reason, we're deepening our partnership with the San Antonio Spurs to expand AI literacy among parents and youth sports coaches across Texas.

Last Sunday, we gathered at the Spurs’ former practice facility for an OpenAI Academy with volunteer parent coaches—exploring how ChatGPT can support coaching, parenting, and everyday life. Explore the video and photos from the inspiring day. (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37C5jMY8W69t95C6lZ3ktN3-q8D8NWZSqW88KxWl8mCpGKW1Vz97j1x9xPDW2vVy4S8jB0C1W6MMpLd6plCx_W1gRJYL5NQgFRW2Prp627jV_GmW4s0S-W4NWKPlW33WpQs57Tq6fW7qds-675RrnqW6_8HM41ZyGn7W7wZw7m6H3gGSW4t2_cy19RW87N8qw_3pnkVM4N8LYnHGb_RMzV63xqQ2ZDmt4W925X1z2Dh26wW3xYnJx7zrTBCVCPF4Q9k_WB7W6vJ_YM2ZNfmMW99vf0X2ZcRR-W3bh3V45k3qCmW7mfqcc67q-kGW9jtKR58zkPQkW5WhCWg8rt9YFW57YlY79c_RhWW2GBX898vNpvRW4GgKDn3NtW0-W4tqwYk8lyn8DW4yp51x40T8yfW1hyqT_1vgbxHW6HvGGs8-mXqmW41PKJ92sBkZqW9cLv_72QL133W5xBFsF1FVYYDW16zv9_2SHBPpf56NGQP04 )

Across the Forum this month, that same commitment to community, access, and real-world application shows up everywhere—from newsrooms, to the track with NASCAR teams (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37j7hjrlW5BWSxg6lZ3lZW4LWDq31K6d-sW96866b7JBXbgW4JJdnm95szKjW8JX2Cn5qlSdKW9lMFqm6sYjnDW7x_f6k4TSBtrW6zQVhp75Q3f1W6dCy7Q3TBnwbW7dj-qd52_D5DW8NJRHp3c5KJcW1lmjV05dscRxW7gz_Dp7bYVWzW72_KZ48d2rGRW8kkSKm8hbwwzW7MRkYQ8DhfkpW7sj7nF1Bg3hbW3DcMPG4hSmzqW6p0Djm71H6chVZLPYl4fJySJW5pY43r3gsXxGW1_NGXm7Hyd8WVxBDYy4jp9rWVt6lzG1Ry0dRW80QLv35dC1gkW75Qh2g7ZpqWFN1K7YBN4g6l3W1kYy5L3NRpLsW7pQK6x6_PbdkW70zBDP1sdbSYW6S3fr78FGx6TVG4s3s2GGPmjW8kMSx24FSMhpW9hkvMT1KkhGfW33VDrY8QcqcYW1nllgb1cNFjQW6qjRdh5KP8YjW1bcGy418y4ZPW5q9ZPS37N9f4W73kLH-5XyKtkN4X_C2nH0GN4W2p2NNc8WTh7fW8RjfTg1FVPMGW3mNPWs6w5jl0W1K9BDD2RLv1RW64g8Qc424yr9W7XCNjQ6__kjGW8M4p7869fwdcW60bDpf2w_PSxVWYV9C79mR-TN9h_SZ1-Ng1Xf3XgB1T04 ) turning experimentation into everyday practice, to labs accelerating scientific discovery (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37W7hjrlW6N2kFb6lZ3lpW6vB4QW71BXMkV5Qzvt9fmMdwW6fBRFP4hFQpsW7C4Tj16vdW02VPKMhj784gfvW2ZRNkW6Fkh_lW6YjhSf16DJf8W5cr6-v40BtY7W6rsRXq22yCwhVVWXF8838LQQV4jDcK2_nJ6hW5lqDq14jqQFKN3R6bRm6ZPC_W393qMC21pSBmW1GBS2D4vPrJgW63DV7B7Wm5PtW3BPSxc4pkyNnW4xlDsD16j1FgW3wkM_C3mPnRWW4yC6bs3CnfxCW48NsD_4zcylyN534SKsgcNq_W5604v46jjpj3VkN7Cx6tpqGXVcV2Tn2d9RJRW7x_Byf89s0DKMgDd3dYnDjpW4SqnXm5DRWmQW7-SpN615dFVdW2QB0h73YsS36W5Cmjfn2BqBzKW8_Z-w2619L29W5RnnX02Cl-xWW7bk1Rw2qFlN_W2BZ1Pd6CmsMJW5nxRr96SNNHZW6yCSDY6TmrM7W3PRxFg4tgNt_VDFZgz8MKtN3N3ST3lcJBXcSW1_j4Ph1808CBW5H6Sg34MnS__VxJbPP2VRLwpW7gdZpP2XvXCMW7gH1-x8wVQbBW12TS8z51k-K1W8HRmn13Q_vBwW8-8Y6h2Tf8KRW5CfSX52Z3vl-W97L-r_7P4CKpW70b21w4hTrcnN4BWCm8x0JRkW5fjsBv3w5ZljW8PmJl42J8lGTf8mX7pg04 ) , to economists sharing new data on AI’s impact on work and the global economy.

Join us this month to Learn, Discuss, and Shape AI in the OpenAI Forum.

Interested in telling your story in the Forum? We’re collecting real-world stories from Forum members about how you’re using ChatGPT responsibly in everyday life, work, and research.  👉Share your story here. (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37W3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3lqW40s7sm1xWx9HW7cWJQs1Q8qDYN3_VB43J4DvpV6KL0m3m7bn-W5c2NmG4d4p-kW4Q5K1S1QhwgjW3mssyt7xlSzNW3FKrFz1sk0VPN5bp6PD7WbRmW8jLqH-4_lJdkW1ws4BZ1R-h_DW8PkgFy4ks3CvW7B_7CN4y6DGrVwWtf87y_YggW8G6s4w3064RHW43rtKD4nHS_NW8HsTx07jyj3wW8cWQh21Ph54sW456PKB1dd0HzW36Tf8C5n3PgzW89TJT64jZD91W7w4Cnz4NfnQhf1NmGvC04 )

Upcoming Community Events

Monday, April 6th @ 11am PT

Virtual Event: As AI capabilities accelerate, so does public interest in how these systems are built, deployed, and governed. In this OpenAI Forum conversation, Sam Altman is joined by Chief Futurist Josh Achiam and researcher Adrien Ecoffet to discuss how OpenAI is approaching the next phase of AI.

This conversation focuses on what comes next: how society keeps pace with rapid technological change, how more people can actively shape the trajectory of AI, and how we build systems that are not just powerful, but widely beneficial, resilient, and aligned with the public interest.

(https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37j7hjrlW5BWSxg6lZ3nwW8XKmW33k-k6tW2KqgWQ96jf6rW1f2Wmh7d0X1PW7qZ95n2v2N0lW6_03Xb26TCSWW2FyV9C37ZpV2W7D248k6gtP3dW3xlWJJ8HlLRDW8Hd-yB435r9pW6XMbM84MMXFBW4HdXPf7jBPh9W7PJ4KX33yGnkN54p8ZSpWVhfVTBPby62zCt-W3Slt513h6VyjW2w1hLM4R6x9JW5QPWZ760ZlylN1VvTCnFQZCmW2tNvSv41fmtjW2RjKjG3YwJBxW2Tq2cb2HP0r6W2ZyR8j2HBrvvW4khkqF2p-JY6W13fjzk80NrzFW5fvtW97bKYxJW330-Z31Q59wjW5R1LHr1NS9TgW50g61S6PQmFPW92D-sR8bgXDDN45cHFzj6nByW6LcJpw7vDtMbW6cx48d3cGMWqW7Cb-Hn8Hv4fhW442Wx-35sLM0W58SwPJ7KFBs6W4-3jzH1lV97fN3BMKb1NP15zW3Jcp_84zTgLtN3Jzlq_lcjKlW84-NH_1HDt5-W4cv1Q71w1SlZW2CBTSX7NbP-fW5jxx5N6xv66JV67nT67bz30YW1v1gsm6MbPm9W7SvhfF3-VXSbW3Shy061DCMpKW486Scv8K3jxPW1Xg4GD4b-D4vVl5CVz17dJkKf1xx7v804 )

Thursday, April 2nd @ 7am PT

Virtual Event: AI is increasingly part of how journalists research, analyze, and tell stories. In this OpenAI Forum session, we will replay a previously recorded in-person conversation from our gathering in Washington D.C. earlier in March, followed by a live Q&A and demo. The conversation brings together journalists, editors, and technologists to discuss how AI is being used across news organizations today, what responsible integration looks like in practice, and how shared guidance and learnings can help ensure these tools strengthen high-quality journalism and serve the public interest.

Register Here
(https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37W7hjrlW6N2kFb6lZ3lCW22G5wN6Cp2WvW8_3K1_79b-GZVfywHz9jMnH5W3xBvJV8Zxn01N2b655gYvSYxW1VcGMB1C_FQWW4Y0ryq2LZhczW3ZjW2D2qB9GFN1mdcXGxK71jW8JzWfb8s7ZnVVC97rC6hzQSBW48KQJJ2KZDQ5W2fVV1F1n_yCKW3z6btZ6z64kJW7x5RzP46VzM1W1wjlHR88r4J1W36CTdS1K9nnvW4mVg6M9d2ztfW8psd7L3wmr7hN86sX0GkQSy7VZnGwZ4gsB1RW51Wzbc8sjtmwW2B7VBR5yKY_rW6KHRsp3r1H51VM2cnr3J-lqDW2NsKpC2H27LrVB93ld7ZBg6_W8JkrdW6khf5TW8lGGCt79_TfMW2Gt12-38nynJW1KGN3T7pB8SrW7H0xD488f1yZW50ZzZ-8QFrygN5jp5zQz_9DLW60c9Xd5pgVRvW6rXdmr3M2sfhV5xQKD4t2-SqW6ythZW3sWgC8W70Q-gF33T06NN7cC_t1J2P1YW87Dl_D9chxjrN2hK3XPynynGW23rpty64tSxKN3wHfNsqWYjlW6QPKC73MpYSzW1hpzWf54CwfzVpnVXH7zm6rZW2dnZM-6KPC1zW70Yj0W3_gsd2N83DfkM5sVjpW3Y8mNH5YlLNYW6cPSGB4H8QYhW4Vc6h85WR98XW1V2_-K5zh_KMdvPMZg04 )

Wednesday, April 8th @ 4:00pm PST

Virtual Event: What does AI look like inside one of the world’s fastest and most data-driven sports? Join the OpenAI Forum for a conversation with NASCAR’s Rich Bowman, AI Operations Specialist, and Derek Thomas, Managing Director of Analytics, and about how the organization is bringing AI into its day-to-day work, including analytics, operations, and workforce enablement. In racing, safety and performance go hand in hand. NASCAR has applied that philosophy to technology for more than 80 years and AI is no different. The organization is moving quickly to explore new capabilities while building the training and guardrails that help teams use these tools responsibly.

(https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37j7hjrlW5BWSxg6lZ3lZW4LWDq31K6d-sW96866b7JBXbgW4JJdnm95szKjW8JX2Cn5qlSdKW9lMFqm6sYjnDW7x_f6k4TSBtrW6zQVhp75Q3f1W6dCy7Q3TBnwbW7dj-qd52_D5DW8NJRHp3c5KJcW1lmjV05dscRxW7gz_Dp7bYVWzW72_KZ48d2rGRW8kkSKm8hbwwzW7MRkYQ8DhfkpW7sj7nF1Bg3hbW3DcMPG4hSmzqW6p0Djm71H6chVZLPYl4fJySJW5pY43r3gsXxGW1_NGXm7Hyd8WVxBDYy4jp9rWVt6lzG1Ry0dRW80QLv35dC1gkW75Qh2g7ZpqWFN1K7YBN4g6l3W1kYy5L3NRpLsW7pQK6x6_PbdkW70zBDP1sdbSYW6S3fr78FGx6TVG4s3s2GGPmjW8kMSx24FSMhpW9hkvMT1KkhGfW33VDrY8QcqcYW1nllgb1cNFjQW6qjRdh5KP8YjW1bcGy418y4ZPW5q9ZPS37N9f4W73kLH-5XyKtkN4X_C2nH0GN4W2p2NNc8WTh7fW8RjfTg1FVPMGW3mNPWs6w5jl0W1K9BDD2RLv1RW64g8Qc424yr9W7XCNjQ6__kjGW8M4p7869fwdcW60bDpf2w_PSxVWYV9C79mR-TN9h_SZ1-Ng1Xf3XgB1T04 )

Wednesday, April 15th @ 12:40pm PST

Virtual Event: Artificial intelligence is rapidly becoming part of everyday life, shaping how people work, learn, and solve problems. But alongside its growing adoption, questions remain about how to measure its economic and broader social impacts. Join OpenAI Chief Economist Ronnie Chatterji, Gregor Schubert, Assistant Professor Of Finance at UCLA Anderson School of Management, and other leading voices from industry and government for a conversation on what OpenAI’s usage data reveals about how the technology is actually being used today and what it means for the future.The conversation will draw on how people rely on OpenAI usage data and broader research to explore how AI is already being integrated into everyday tasks, workplaces, and learning environments. Stay tuned for ongoing releases of additional data, research, and reports as part of the OpenAI Economic Research team’s ongoing effort to share insights into the ways AI is being used around the world.

(https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37j7hjrlW5BWSxg6lZ3pYW1v_Dnj25DzdQW8WTLv75kWF1cW3bWCWD88L6wPW56zqp95KG-_dW72mw4S91C1-zN2Sp29SBqjKZW7FZ7Wr3LtLwNW2mv7VK5G7CpsW2Hq6Q95Xw2htW6xZBmF2bj-XSW6fYyTj5qV38MW8kQC7m3749bDW4_qFgf3WNX8TW3ldTY57gZqQnV7K5CK3QCQhjW9csWtB3Q8PtNW2XddfR2JXnCVW26zSz17M81yfW43f7kf73sSJYW5N-zN23Z3VbXW6pJ1K67PxKH2V2LHQ_8Dk_dHW95Fxyx6NvG01W85G-P33yVhlHW554R5789XQWgVbZP797__WQqW2klymb4RNSTDN24b1qn6_fmPW2FKd7v5Q6MN4W3VSn2Z437x_kW4nHXSx7f_MZjN1KJ6f_FFYR2VRQ0mC7x362sW7RVlmW85lfCBW833ckf4cSwyTW49zqKx9b1mcxW8XvqZQ8fNFxjW50TyJ17GplLgN1twYrSbW_DwW3hnDX-8Kp04tVQmZwK5DlTFhW3h3Rc07-1v-CVFDCCC19kR7PW3yTHC_70YqvwW7lfH0r7fpr8QW3RGQSW3HLTTyW78gVx-87plk0W6VT3NF96j2pGW2c3ghh3rdVfgW8_GCY720pH_Ff3XqKCM04 )

Wednesday, April 22 @ 1:00pm PST

Virtual: What if we could predict disease before it starts, tailor treatments to each person, and discover new medicines in a fraction of the time? Grace Zheng and Xin Jin, co-founders of PerturbAI, will show how we’re moving from simply mapping the brain to actually predicting how it works. In partnership with OpenAI, they’ve built one of the largest brain datasets ever created, capturing activity across 8 million cells, and are using AI to uncover the rules that drive biology. The result: a new era of healthcare that is more proactive, more precise, and far more effective.

On Demand Content

Replay: AI is already changing how people work. On March 18, 2026, OpenAI hosted the Participation Economy Forum to bring together labor, education, policy, and industry leaders to talk about how AI is beginning to shape the labor market and what that means for American workers. In this conversation, OpenAI Chief Economist Dr. Ronnie Chatterji and Deputy Secretary of Labor Keith Sonderling discuss the need for public-private coordination around AI literacy training, apprenticeships and rapid reskilling can help Americans build relevant skills and adapt to an AI-driven economy.

Watch the Recording Here
(https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37C5jMY8W69t95C6lZ3nYW20K_995nt_wsW5MxBsq5Lr2ZdN1lrRNN5WYbMW7KytpK92LNjCW4ylzk57Nf6wKW1Dy45l5XjrlsW32QLVR8BrnwcW4SDxjn43YjVsW1SJygp5d0xQZW6ZkrPd6Cg-pSW1r729t45fxRJVBvB8t44W3wsW6cz__h8P9ZxKW3hW0d474B8wXN6WP7ZCnRHM0VlpVjp63YKDvW5x8HGJ4TXFytV8llB_11tJSfW54lSs_1t76ntVp_YT-3vD6lsMw9bZ5ZMtgYW335z3-3r1PDjW4YgqrX1Ybv8wW31RJ3J3H0B0-W90RYMQ6YgPgqW1MmMf31dg5SZW7bCndQ85TPQCW41sm1t4kV_NHW7bfPkH67bC7CW6vPRng8jj9MjW7sdX2R677p-7W5GXjgk2B0DGjW5ZLNdd3qL3j3N8PfHSWRx9q-W5JFGBf75xzlgW3FrVF41kBFX7f5hSMLz04 )

Replay: AI is beginning to reshape how people work across industries. On March 18, 2026, OpenAI's Vice President of Global Policy Ann O'Leary sat down for a conversation with Randi Weingarten, President of the American Federation of Teachers, and Sean McGarvey, President of North America's Building Trades Union (NABTU) about how workers and institutions are responding to that shift, and what it will take to prepare millions of Americans for an AI-driven economy. The conversation focused on the importance of moving from resistance to adoption, with both leaders emphasizing training, certification, and lifelong learning as critical tools for helping workers adapt. They highlighted the growing demand for accessible AI education and the role trusted institutions can play in guiding workers through this transition. At the same time, the discussion underscored the need for stronger coordination across the public and private sectors. As AI accelerates change, investments in reskilling, clear guardrails, and policies that address displacement will be essential to ensure workers can adapt and continue to thrive.

Watch the Recording Here
(https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk38v3lcq-W7Y8-PT6lZ3pGVHgBc743K-z1W87ZjTC783WGWW1zgGQW4dZytXW1ZbXRJ8ZXXbRN8d5RSLCv7qhV_7PbX7Bnl1rW5vfjfc3g8PHBW552gtn8ZTY7kW3R17zc7-4s19W23QwYq7w6R7qW5-JTgp6khDz3W6WdbDL6GYRCHW6QXClp1lyS6mW2scsk72cnK99W6Q8-5M5FSjwjW6GYCJM10gg3ZW5LFRQn1P06CqW6PKTRn74DH5JN4nDFjv7sRWhW6Gj2Jd1zNWvnW5x5-pS7f5zB4W2PHNQK1J6hFNN34MlRbb4bScN33t-6j2fydHW5RwxxS3MXcyWW64fgQ752dlqwf1Kmd8s04 )

About the OpenAI Forum

The OpenAI Forum is a vibrant global community of experts committed to advancing OpenAI's mission: building artificial general intelligence (AGI) that benefits everyone.

Since 2023, the OpenAI Forum has been building an ecosystem of collaborators with the intention of forging impactful partnerships, exchanging resources and ideas, and amplifying AI use cases working to solve humanity's most challenging problems.

What to Expect:

The Forum offers regular virtual and in-person programming—including expert panels, OpenAI-led research talks, ChatGPT tutorials, networking events, and dinner mixers at OpenAI offices. You’ll meet trailblazers across disciplines: from astronomers and neuroscientists to artists, public sector leaders, economists, and more. You’re not just an attendee—you’re a welcomed guest at every event.

Network of Experts:

Meet and collaborate with the people operating at the highest expression of expertise in their field.

Public Resources & Replays: Anyone in the world is welcome to join the OpenAI Forum as a guest. Guests can attend live streamed events and explore and share our growing archive of past talks, essays, and learning resources.

The community is composed of 2 tiers of engagement:

- Guests: All you have to do is sign up and activate your profile by validating your email.
- Members: in order to become a full member of the OpenAI Forum, you need to

- Complete your profile, including a headshot, and a short bio section.
- Demonstrate expertise or significant level of experience in your discipline, or area of practice.
- Register for an upcoming event.

Who Qualifies as an Expert?

We define expertise as deep insight in a specific area—gained through study, practice, creative work, lived experience, or leadership. We welcome individuals shaping their fields through research, storytelling, policy, entrepreneurship, education, or advocacy. Credentials matter, but so do grassroots leadership, interdisciplinary work, and nontraditional paths. Expertise comes in many forms, across identities, geographies, and disciplines.

Instructions to update your profile and be considered for full membership:

- Login to forum.openai.com (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/MW-SWLv6P3pW5cy-6F8vBcMJW2gkVcw5Mn9MxN3Gk37W3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3nfVzt7DB7YyqL7W3HZ5M51QGxNCN2jB3QcJlGJ2W6z9XCF57H3p_W3rdmR075JWBPW2bQlnl3DL1SpN6Wk_ycc8NF3W89rf_c1qnk7sW2vdQ8z8fXNbdW9dgdhR5V27RzW7GZrVf2k-z1nW5q2R4T2_HQ67W8VGrB47Fn_42W5Z_Ypw8HqQf9W6FQtLb5q98YCW2NlF7X4qHxMcW6mjlL13qVMj3W8CXX0360y_c4N1VT8Zd7YJr6W3RwTbC3cLlkPVsHzhT7gHQR5N98sdt0YnVkyf7F--VK04 ) with the email from which you are receiving this newsletter

subject: Thank You for Joining Our Caterpillar Intern AMA and Onboarding Updates!
body:
Good afternoon all,

Thank you to everyone who joined our Ask Me Anything (AMA) session for incoming Commercial and Digital summer interns and a special thank you to our previous interns who shared their time, honesty, and great advice! It was energizing to see the thoughtful questions and open discussion, and we hope you left feeling excited and confident as you prepare for your internship at Caterpillar.

I have attached the updates that were shared and below is a recap of some of the great questions and insights shared during the AMA session:

Working Hours & Office Setup

  *   Intern working hours vary by team, but many follow a fairly standard schedule (often around 8:00 a.m.– 4:00 p.m.). It is important to align with your team’s norms.
  *   Most interns will have assigned desks, while some offices use hotel seating, which may encourage coming in a bit earlier and leaving earlier as well.

What Best Prepares You for Full-Time Work

  *   Come in open-minded. The panel shared that they didn’t know much about the company initially, and that is okay.
  *   Caterpillar is invested in your growth and development, regardless of role.
  *   Key skills developed during the internship:
     *   Time management and pacing projects
     *   Being proactive and curious
     *   Offering solutions and taking initiative
  *   Most importantly: don’t be afraid to ask questions.

What Interns Often Underestimate

  *   Mistakes are part of learning. Don’t be afraid to take on work and challenges.
  *   Show your willingness to learn, say “yes” to opportunities, and attend networking events.
  *   You don’t need to go above and beyond all the time to be recognized.
  *   Imposter syndrome is very common. Many have felt it, even full-time employees.

Skills & Tools to Brush Up On

  *   Ask your manager what platforms or tools your team uses so you can prepare ahead of time.
  *   Managers expect interns to build skills during the program and will provide resources such as LinkedIn Learning, Workday Learning, and other Caterpillar tools.

Showcasing Your Work

  *   Interns will have the opportunity to present or demo their work at the end of the internship.
  *   A strong tip: take notes throughout the summer on what you worked on, what you learned, and the impact you made. It makes your final presentation much easier.

Building Relationships Beyond Your Team

  *   Ask intentionally: “Who would you suggest I connect with if I’m interested in X, Y, or Z?”
  *   Join Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) and sign up for mentoring opportunities.
  *   Spend time in the office when possible. Even if your team members are in different locations, there is value in in-person learning and exposure.
  *   If you enjoy a speaker or presentation, don’t hesitate to send a quick message or connection invite to learn more.

We are so excited to welcome you this summer and can’t wait to see what you accomplish. Thank you again for your engagement, curiosity, and enthusiasm. We look forward to connecting with you soon!

Megan Tomala

Caterpillar, Inc. | Digital, Technology & Distribution (DT&D)
501 SW Jefferson Peoria, IL 61630 | 309-633-8507

StrengthsFinder34 Signature Themes
Arranger | Connectedness | Strategic | Achiever | Positivity



Caterpillar: Confidential Green

body:
Dear Cristina Le,

Cartus recognizes the importance of timely reimbursement of your relocation expenses. On Mar 27, 2026, your Lump Sum Request 3/27/2026 12:04:57 AM expense report was successfully submitted.

If you have forgotten to attach receipts/documents that are required for reimbursement, you may log into CartusOnline to review your submitted report. While the report status is 'Pending Audit', you will have the opportunity to attach additional receipts/documents to avoid delay or non payment.

subject: Lump Sum Ready for Request
body:
You may request your Lump Sum!

Dear Cristina Le,


In accordance with your relocation policy, you are now eligible to receive your Lump Sum payment in the amount of 9296.50 USD. When you are ready to have the funds sent to you, you will use CartusOnline® to instruct us of how you would like the funds disbursed.


  • Login to CartusOnline and select the task Request Your Lump Sum. You will be guided through the simple steps.


  • You are welcome to request the funds at any time. However Caterpillar may stipulate an expiration date for your benefits, so please refer to the details in your policy guidelines to ensure eligibility.


  • If you would like funds to be sent to you via direct deposit, please have all your banking information available. You will want to confirm the instructions with your bank in advance to assure no delays in payment. If you would like further instructions, please refer to your CartusOnline document center for the document entitled How to Set Up Reimbursement Payment Options.


  • Once you submit your request, you may check the status at any time on CartusOnline.


I am available to assist you should you have any questions.

Warm regards,

Carolina Yamada

Cartus
Tel: 55 11 2395 8170
Carolina.Yamada@cartus.com
www.cartus.com

You don't often get email from cristinathoale@gmail.com<mailto:cristinathoale@gmail.com>. Learn why this is important<https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification>

Dear ARC at Old Colony Team,

I hope this message finds you well! My name is Cristina, and I signed a lease for a studio at the ARC for my upcoming summer internship early in December. I just wanted to kindly follow up regarding my preference for the larger (1B) studio layout.

A few months ago, I called the office to ask about adjusting my lease to a 1b1ba, and the kind employee I spoke with let me know that wouldn’t be possible since my lease was already signed and processed. I completely understood, and I was super appreciative to hear that my preference for the 1B layout would be noted. As the summer approaches, I just wanted to check in to follow up on that preference.

I understand that unit assignments depend on availability and want to keep my expectations realistic, but I would be incredibly grateful to secure this particular layout if possible. I’ll be relocating from California on my own and I've been a bit nervous about the move as it gets closer, so I greatly appreciate how kind the ARC team has been to me!

Thank you very much for your time and assistance. I look forward to your reply.

Best regards,
Cristina Le

* TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Earn up to 100,000 Bonus Points: You can receive 15,000 bonus points for each friend you refer through this Refer-A-Friend offer (up to 7), who is approved for the card. Maximum bonus points accumulation for this offer is 100,000 bonus points. If 10 or more of your friends use the referral URL within a month to apply, at Chase’s discretion you may not receive any bonus points for the friends who apply that month and are approved for the card. We may provide you with future opportunities to earn bonus points by referring your friends to any participating Chase Sapphire Credit Card. Participating Chase Sapphire Cards include: Chase Sapphire Preferred and Chase Sapphire Reserve. You may earn bonus points up to the stated maximum for each offer, however, please note that if you take advantage
 of multiple opportunities within one calendar year that the maximum number of bonus points that can be awarded from all referral offers is 100,000 per calendar year. In the event the bonus points you earn from all referral offers exceeds the 100,000 per calendar year limit, you may be awarded a partial amount to keep your total bonus at 100,000 bonus points. For example if you have already accumulated 90,000 bonus points from refer-a-friend offers that calendar year and the maximum is 100,000 bonus points per calendar year, then the next award will be 10,000 even if the offer states 15,000. The value of this offer or program may result in miscellaneous income received from Chase and Chase may be required to send you, and file with the IRS, a Form 1099-MISC (Miscellaneous Information) or
 Form 1042-S (Foreign Person’s U.S. Source Income Subject to Withholding) for the year in which you participate and are awarded the benefits of the offer/program. You are responsible for any tax liability related to participating in the offer/program. Please consult your own tax advisor if you have any questions about how this could affect your taxes. This offer is subject to change and may be cancelled at any time without notice. To be eligible for this bonus offer, account must be open and not in default at the time of fulfillment. Please allow up to 8 weeks after each new cardmember’s approval for bonus points to post to your account. Your bonus will appear on your statement as “Refer-A-Friend bonus.” You are not eligible for a referral bonus if you use your own referral URL to apply
 for a different card. Due to the confidential nature of the credit approval process, we cannot disclose information on who has applied, nor will you be notified whether we approve or decline any application(s) from the individual(s) you refer. From: Cristina Le <cristinathoale@gmail.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 9:14 PM
To: Arc at Old Colony Info (Live) <live@ArcAtOldColony.com>
Subject: Summer Lease Studio Layout Preference

You don't often get email from cristinathoale@gmail.com. Learn why this is important<https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification>

Dear ARC at Old Colony Team,

I hope this message finds you well! My name is Cristina, and I signed a lease for a studio at the ARC for my upcoming summer internship early in December. I just wanted to kindly follow up regarding my preference for the larger (1B) studio layout.

A few months ago, I called the office to ask about adjusting my lease to a 1b1ba, and the kind employee I spoke with let me know that wouldn’t be possible since my lease was already signed and processed. I completely understood, and I was super appreciative to hear that my preference for the 1B layout would be noted. As the summer approaches, I just wanted to check in to follow up on that preference.

I understand that unit assignments depend on availability and want to keep my expectations realistic, but I would be incredibly grateful to secure this particular layout if possible. I’ll be relocating from California on my own and I've been a bit nervous about the move as it gets closer, so I greatly appreciate how kind the ARC team has been to me!

Thank you very much for your time and assistance. I look forward to your reply.

Best regards,
Cristina Le

subject: Summer Lease Studio Layout Preference
body:
Dear ARC at Old Colony Team,

I hope this message finds you well! My name is Cristina, and I signed a
lease for a studio at the ARC for my upcoming summer internship early in
December. I just wanted to kindly follow up regarding my preference for the
larger (1B) studio layout.

A few months ago, I called the office to ask about adjusting my lease to a
1b1ba, and the kind employee I spoke with let me know that wouldn’t be
possible since my lease was already signed and processed. I completely
understood, and I was super appreciative to hear that my preference for the
1B layout would be noted. As the summer approaches, I just wanted to check
in to follow up on that preference.

I understand that unit assignments depend on availability and want to keep
my expectations realistic, but I would be incredibly grateful to secure
this particular layout if possible. I’ll be relocating from California on
my own and I've been a bit nervous about the move as it gets closer, so I
greatly appreciate how kind the ARC team has been to me!

Thank you very much for your time and assistance. I look forward to your
reply.

Best regards,
Cristina Le

Check the " https://rttrack.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=t5df33eb9,f4cf71c,20e9b59 FAFSA ® Deadlines " page for more information. subject: A friendly reminder to contribute to your IRA
body:
Fidelity Investments, Inc.

 
 
 

 
 



 
 Your future will thank you.
https://click.fidelityinvestments.com/u/? 
View in a browser
https://click.fidelityinvestments.com/u/?%3D 
Cristina le

https://click.fidelityinvestments.com/u/?%3D 
Log in 
 
 
 
 
 Invest in your future

Contribute to your IRA by April 15, 2026,* the federal IRA contribution deadline, through our mobile app or a simple online transfer―and experience tax-advantaged savings.


Maximizing your contributions each year can help give your savings the most potential for growth. For example, if you contribute $7,000 today, it could potentially grow to $74,736 in 35 years.†
https://click.fidelityinvestments.com/u/? 
See how your additional contribution amounts could impact potential growth over time .


Remember your contribution limit for 2025 is $7,000.‡






 
https://click.fidelityinvestments.com/u/?%3D 
Contribute now

Learn more  about IRA contribution eligibility and limits.

Contact Us|

Fidelity Mobile(R)For your security and privacy, please do not reply to this email. Our 
https://click.fidelityinvestments.com/u/? 
Fidelity Assistant  may be able to help you find answers to many questions.


To stop receiving account service communications by email, 
https://click.fidelityinvestments.com/u/? 
log in  to your Fidelity account and opt out under “Account updates, appointments, and service-related messages.”Investing involves risk, including risk of loss.

*Contribution deadlines by states may vary. Please refer to the IRS.gov and search for “deadlines” to check your state’s tax filing deadline.  


†This hypothetical example assumes the following: (1) one annual $7,000 IRA contribution made on January 1 of the first year, (2) annual rate of return of 7% , and (3) no taxes on any earnings within the IRA. The ending values do not reflect taxes, fees or inflation. If they did, amounts would be lower. Earnings and pre-tax (deductible) contributions from a traditional IRA are subject to taxes when withdrawn. Earnings distributed from Roth IRAs are income tax free provided certain requirements are met. IRA distributions before age 59 1/2 may also be subject to a 10% penalty. Systematic investing does not ensure a profit and does not protect against loss in a declining market.
 Consider your current and anticipated investment horizon when making an investment decision, as the illustration may not reflect this. The assumed rate of return used in this example is not guaranteed. Investments that have potential for a 7% annual rate of return also come with risk of loss. This hypothetical does not reflect the impact that minimum required distributions from a traditional IRA could have if you turn 73 during the time period.

 

‡The contribution limit noted above is for the 2025 tax year and is based on your age as of 12/31/2025.

Important Information

1Service Fee: Chase Total Checking has $0 Monthly Service Fee when you have any one of the following during each statement period: Option #1: $500+ in qualifying electronic deposits; or, Option #2: $1,500+ balance at the beginning of each day; or, Option #3: $5,000+ average beginning day balance in this account, or in combination with any linked qualifying personal deposits or investments; or, Option #4: Link this account to a qualifying checking account. Otherwise a $15 Monthly Service Fee will apply. Qualifying electronic deposits include payments from your employer or government entities (benefits, tax refunds, etc.), made using the ACH network, Real Time Payment (RTP(R)), FedNow(R) Service, or third-party services that facilitate payments to your debit card using the Visa(R) or
 Mastercard(R) network. Transactions such as Zelle(R), cash, checks, wire transfers, and interest payments do not count as qualifying electronic deposits. Qualifying personal deposits include balances in Chase First CheckingSM, Chase SavingsSM, Certificate of Deposit (CDs), certain Chase Retirement CDs, or certain Chase Retirement Money Market accounts. Qualifying investments include balances in certain investment and annuity products offered through JPMorgan Chase & Co. and its affiliates and agencies. We use daily balances for most investment products. Some third-party providers report balances on a periodic basis and we will use the most current balance reported to calculate the average beginning day balance. Balances in 529 plans, donor-advised funds, and certain retirement plan
 investment accounts do not qualify. Qualifying linked checking accounts include Chase Premier Plus CheckingSM, Chase Sapphire(R) Checking, Chase Private Client CheckingSM, J.P. Morgan Private Client Checking Plus, J.P. Morgan Classic Checking, Chase Performance Business Checking(R), or Chase Performance Business Checking With Interest(R). For personal accounts, there must be a common owner. For business accounts, the owner of the linked personal account must be a direct owner of the business and signer on the business account.

Chase SavingsSM has $0 Monthly Service Fee when you have any one of the following during each statement period: Option #1: $300+ balance at the beginning of each day; or, Option #2: $25+ in total Autosave or other repeating automatic transfers from your personal Chase checking account (available only through chase.com 
 or Chase Mobile(R)); or, Option #3: Link this account to a qualifying checking account; or, Option #4: Link this account to a Chase College CheckingSM account that is enrolled in Overdraft Protection; or, Option #5: An account owner who is under the age of 18. Otherwise a $5 Monthly Service Fee will apply. Qualifying linked checking accounts include Chase Premier Plus CheckingSM, Chase Sapphire(R) Checking, Chase Private Client CheckingSM, J.P. Morgan Private Client Checking Plus, or J.P. Morgan Classic Checking. For personal accounts, there must be a common owner.

Checking and Savings: New and converted accounts will not be charged a Monthly Service Fee for at least the first two statement periods. After that, the Monthly Service Fee will apply unless you meet one of the ways to avoid the Monthly Service Fee each statement period (if applicable).


 Product terms subject to change. For more information, please see a banker or visit 
https://click.mcmap.chase.com/?%3D%3D 
chase.com/checking  or 
https://click.mcmap.chase.com/?%3D%3D 
chase.com/savings . 

Bonus/Account Information: Checking offer is not available to existing Chase checking customers. Savings offer is not available to existing Chase savings customers. Both offers are not available to those whose accounts have been closed within 90 days or closed with a negative balance within the last 3 years. You can receive only one new checking and one new savings account opening related bonus every two years from the last coupon enrollment date and only one bonus per account. Coupon is good for one-time use. To receive the checking bonus: 1) Open a new Chase Total Checking account, which is subject to approval; and 2) Have your direct deposit made into the new checking account within 90 days of coupon enrollment. Your direct deposit needs to be an electronic deposit of your paycheck,
 pension or government benefits (such as Social Security) from your employer or the government. Person to Person payments (such as Zelle(R)) are not considered a direct deposit. Micro-deposits do not qualify as a direct deposit for the bonus. Micro-deposits are small deposits, typically less than $1, that are sent to your account to verify it is the correct account. After you have completed all the above checking requirements, we'll deposit the bonus in your new account within 15 days. To receive the savings bonus: 1) Open a new Chase SavingsSM account, which is subject to approval; 2) Deposit a total of $15,000 or more in new money into the new savings account within 30 days of coupon enrollment. The new money cannot be funds held by you at Chase or its affiliates; and 3) Maintain at
 least a $15,000 balance in the new savings account for 90 days from the coupon enrollment. After you have completed all the above savings requirements, we'll deposit the bonus in your new account within 15 days. To receive the extra bonus: You must open the checking and savings account at the same time and complete all requirements above for both the checking bonus and savings bonus. After you have completed all requirements, we will deposit the remaining bonus due in your new account within 15 days. To receive any of the above bonuses, the enrolled account must not be closed or restricted at the time of payout. Eligibility may be limited based on account ownership. The Annual Percentage Yield (APY) for Chase SavingsSM, effective as of 01/02/2026 
, is 0.01% for all balances in all states. Interest rates are variable and subject to change. Additionally, fees may reduce earnings on the account. Bonuses are considered interest and will be reported on IRS Form 1099-INT (or Form 1042-S, if applicable).

subject: Ingram Micro’s Second Batch of 2026 Summer Internships Now Open!
body:
[A blue and black logo  Description automatically generated]<https://corp.ingrammicro.com/>



[Feature image alt text copy 600x250]
2026 Summer Internship Program





Ingram Micro’s second batch of 2026 Summer Internship roles is now live!

Review and apply to the openings here: Ingram Micro's 2026 Summer Internship Postings<https://careers.ingrammicro.com/en/jobs/?search=intern&country=United+States+of+America&pagesize=20#results>

This batch of postings includes opportunities focused on Information Systems, Digital Innovation, Data, and other technology-driven functions across our Irvine, CA and Buffalo, NY offices.

If you previously applied to one of the internship roles in our first batch, you are still welcome to review these new openings and apply to any positions that align with your interests and qualifications.

Summer Internship Program Details:

Ingram Micro’s Summer Internship Program is a 10-week, hybrid, paid opportunity starting in June, offering hands-on experience in Marketing, Finance, Information Systems, and more. Expect immersive learning, professional growth opportunities, and networking events that shape both your personal and professional journey. Explore more details through our Early Career Programs<https://careers.ingrammicro.com/en/locations/north-america/united-states/early-careers/> site.

Important Dates:

• March: Applications open, with postings remaining live for at least 3 days. After that, postings may be removed at any time due to applicant volume. We recommend submitting your application as soon as possible.
• March - April: Recruiters and hiring managers will review applications and reach out to candidates on a rolling basis. Interview timelines may vary by position, but candidates selected for interviews may expect to hear from us by the end of April. We kindly ask that you keep that timing in mind as we work through the process.
• June 1 - August 7: First cohort start & end date
• June 15 - August 21: Second cohort start & end date

We look forward to reviewing your application.

*You are receiving this email because you signed up for Ingram Micro’s Early Career Programs mailing list. If you expressed interest in our Global Platform Technology Fresh Grad Rotation Program (for upcoming Software Engineering/Computer Science-related grads), a separate email will be sent regarding that program later this spring.

If you no longer wish to receive notifications like this, please reply to this email with "Unsubscribe".












This message may contain confidential and proprietary information. If it has been sent to you in error, please reply to notify the sender immediately and delete this message. To manage your promotional email preferences, please visit the My Communications<https://now.ingrammicro.com/subscription.html> page.

Otherwise a $15 Monthly Service Fee will apply. Qualifying electronic deposits include payments from your employer or government entities (benefits, tax refunds, etc.), made using the ACH network, Real Time Payment (RTP(R)), FedNow(R) Service, or third-party services that facilitate payments to your debit card using the Visa(R) or
 Mastercard(R) network. Transactions such as Zelle(R), cash, checks, wire transfers, and interest payments do not count as qualifying electronic deposits. Qualifying personal deposits include balances in Chase First CheckingSM, Chase SavingsSM, Certificate of Deposit (CDs), certain Chase Retirement CDs, or certain Chase Retirement Money Market accounts. Qualifying investments include balances in certain investment and annuity products offered through JPMorgan Chase & Co. and its affiliates and agencies. We use daily balances for most investment products. Some third-party providers report balances on a periodic basis and we will use the most current balance reported to calculate the average beginning day balance. Balances in 529 plans, donor-advised funds, and certain retirement plan
 investment accounts do not qualify. Qualifying linked checking accounts include Chase Premier Plus CheckingSM, Chase Sapphire(R) Checking, Chase Private Client CheckingSM, J.P. Morgan Private Client Checking Plus, J.P. Morgan Classic Checking, Chase Performance Business Checking(R), or Chase Performance Business Checking With Interest(R). For personal accounts, there must be a common owner. For business accounts, the owner of the linked personal account must be a direct owner of the business and signer on the business account.

Chase SavingsSM has $0 Monthly Service Fee when you have any one of the following during each statement period: Option #1: $300+ balance at the beginning of each day; or, Option #2: $25+ in total Autosave or other repeating automatic transfers from your personal Chase checking account (available only through chase.com 
 or Chase Mobile(R)); or, Option #3: Link this account to a qualifying checking account; or, Option #4: Link this account to a Chase College CheckingSM account that is enrolled in Overdraft Protection; or, Option #5: An account owner who is under the age of 18. Otherwise a $5 Monthly Service Fee will apply. Qualifying linked checking accounts include Chase Premier Plus CheckingSM, Chase Sapphire(R) Checking, Chase Private Client CheckingSM, J.P. Morgan Private Client Checking Plus, or J.P. Morgan Classic Checking. For personal accounts, there must be a common owner.

Checking and Savings: New and converted accounts will not be charged a Monthly Service Fee for at least the first two statement periods. After that, the Monthly Service Fee will apply unless you meet one of the ways to avoid the Monthly Service Fee each statement period (if applicable).

 
 Product terms subject to change. For more information, please see a banker or visit 
https://click.mcmap.chase.com/? 
chase.com/checking  or 
https://click.mcmap.chase.com/? 
chase.com/savings . 

Bonus/Account Information: Checking offer is not available to existing Chase checking customers. Savings offer is not available to existing Chase savings customers. Both offers are not available to those whose accounts have been closed within 90 days or closed with a negative balance within the last 3 years. You can receive only one new checking and one new savings account opening related bonus every two years from the last coupon enrollment date and only one bonus per account. Coupon is good for one-time use. To receive the checking bonus: 1) Open a new Chase Total Checking account, which is subject to approval; and 2) Have your direct deposit made into the new checking account within 90 days of coupon enrollment. Your direct deposit needs to be an electronic deposit of your paycheck,
 pension or government benefits (such as Social Security) from your employer or the government. Person to Person payments (such as Zelle(R)) are not considered a direct deposit. Micro-deposits do not qualify as a direct deposit for the bonus. Micro-deposits are small deposits, typically less than $1, that are sent to your account to verify it is the correct account. After you have completed all the above checking requirements, we'll deposit the bonus in your new account within 15 days. To receive the savings bonus: 1) Open a new Chase SavingsSM account, which is subject to approval; 2) Deposit a total of $15,000 or more in new money into the new savings account within 30 days of coupon enrollment. The new money cannot be funds held by you at Chase or its affiliates; and 3) Maintain at
 least a $15,000 balance in the new savings account for 90 days from the coupon enrollment. After you have completed all the above savings requirements, we'll deposit the bonus in your new account within 15 days. To receive the extra bonus: You must open the checking and savings account at the same time and complete all requirements above for both the checking bonus and savings bonus. After you have completed all requirements, we will deposit the remaining bonus due in your new account within 15 days. To receive any of the above bonuses, the enrolled account must not be closed or restricted at the time of payout. Eligibility may be limited based on account ownership. The Annual Percentage Yield (APY) for Chase SavingsSM, effective as of 01/02/2026 
, is 0.01% for all balances in all states. Interest rates are variable and subject to change. Additionally, fees may reduce earnings on the account. Bonuses are considered interest and will be reported on IRS Form 1099-INT (or Form 1042-S, if applicable).
