subject: March Career News from NAVSEA
body:
Empowered to Make a Difference: Meet Jay Patel
Meet Jay Patel, a Contract Specialist who has spent the last seven and a half years contributing to NAVSEA’s mission with purpose and passion. After joining through the Naval Acquisition Development Program, Jay found a collaborative, mission‑driven culture that empowered him to grow—from analyst to contracting professional trusted with high‑visibility projects. His rotational experiences, including serving as an Action Officer for NAVSEA’s Executive Director, gave him an enterprise-level perspective and the confidence to lead major procurements that directly support the warfighter. Jay says the ability to shape his own career path has strengthened both his work-life balance and his impact. Read more about Jay’s journey - https://u3445751.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.dRmKBBepCL5WdMYvln7I9a1le3KVGwk-2BVlPHDHmbHXCmTKhxAILfjNo-2BRMxX4loGWs67VLa6-2Bqk3dNVgt1Li-2FJoSXvS3i0uxNNyXSawAabglYLLTKvNapxOz-2BkxEWjKk1Gha_wC5ozFR3fdJxOIYV8k7pfvLhj7Y4IzIFYHx6APv06-2Fj5awj7y7UZ-2FYUfo7mxh7pjCz4ynHspPMyNhNqik3rYry0LS6EURofjAoEXZ9glcxoY3fsLHoWALdqy5QATIdrsZieWa8YPR0hj9mD7ORoWtmgfZfq-2FZxS6J4MjOKUEJwXeF77HA3IZHegkycsuqWNImMUL6L-2B0fwmJnGILbKv9c6QdPKaNwURJ39QhXehIQA5E6uIqIfMN7TUi1ZNwFNF12N-2BketG0Us-2F8zkWofF1CFpPjq3gRzemLL-2BVbjo7CMnLPtuRu7isVL-2Fwta5oECMCPdoupS7kWsQ-2Bq7jQQQiwMq5SW0zipjmCoZ0btJ2C0ON-2FLf-2BEgucN-2FwNY0eWq7eD70EONCQNbtxIwdqurOp7QaZg-3D-3D  and advice for future NAVSEA professionals in the full story.
 
Spring Forward With a NAVSEA Career

subject: Caterpillar - Cleared to Hire!
body:
Good morning,

Congratulations! You have successfully completed the pre-employment onboarding process. Please see the attached roadmap that shows the next steps of what you can expect in Caterpillar's onboarding process.

Action Items:

  *   If you have not already, please send your transcripts to me by April 1st. Unofficial transcripts are accepted - please be sure your completed Fall 2025 classes and in progress Spring 2026 classes are included. Please send transcripts to Lauren.Southwood@cat.com<mailto:Lauren.Southwood@cat.com>

Please reach out if you have any questions!



Cheers,

Lauren Southwood
Digital Talent Acquisition Partner

subject: Caterpillar Intern Newsletter : March 2026
body:
[cid:image001.png@01DCB6BB.536E9D50]
BUILDING BEYOND
Caterpillar is changing the way the future is built. For more than a century, we’ve built a better, more sustainable world. As interns, you’re an important part of what comes next. This experience is about building real skills, making connections and contributing ideas that matter. During your internship, you’ll face challenges, explore opportunities and make an impact. You’ll grow professionally, and we’ll learn from you along the way.

Below are some key milestones to expect as you get closer to your start date.

[cid:image002.png@01DCB6BB.536E9D50]

SECURE YOUR HOUSING
Caterpillar offers relocation benefits to help offset housing costs and support smart financial decisions. Check the attached relocation roadmap for an overview of the process and tips to get started.
Our relocation partner, Cartus, will contact you after you’ve completed your pre-employment steps (background check, drug screen, etc.). They are reaching out to interns by start date, so thank you for your patience.
Get a Head Start. You don’t need to wait for Cartus to begin your search:

  *   Research early: Compare cost, availability, location, amenities and short-term housing options. Cartus recommendations are a starting point—not a full list.
  *   Secure housing ASAP: Availability is limited, and prices may increase closer to your start date. Your offer letter can serve as proof of employment if needed.
Connect with your Cartus consultant if you have questions or run into housing availability issues.

GET ‘ORIENTED’ TO CATERPILLAR
We can't wait to kick off our summer internship program with our 2026 Corporate Intern Orientation on Tuesday, May 19.
We’re excited to share that a very special leader will be joining us at Orientation this year.
While we’re keeping the identity under wraps for now, here’s what we can tell you:
✅They play a key role in shaping our company’s future
✅ They’re passionate about students & graduates talent like you!
On the next newsletter, we’ll be dropping more clues. Think you can guess who it is?

LIFE AT CATERPILLAR – INTERN SPOTLIGHTS!
Ever wondered what it’s really like to intern at Caterpillar? Take a look at these LinkedIn spotlights from last year’s interns—and maybe get inspired to post on LinkedIn yourself!

  *   Logan<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/caterpillar-inc_teamcaterpillar-iamcaterpillar-activity-7387103037200392193-mPS6/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAABbMMp0BMPQyytACeIYoXSsV_PVqk3KJzLo>
  *   Rezin<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/caterpillar-inc_iamcaterpillar-activity-7322597981763891202-gJcf>
  *   Samantha<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/caterpillar-inc_day-in-the-life-of-samantha-an-hr-intern-activity-7371158021881737216-urad>
  *   Ashley<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/caterpillar-inc_teamcaterpillar-iamcaterpillar-activity-7359538933061353472-qAQM>

SUBMIT A SELFIE VIDEO TODAY!
Be featured in a video during Intern Orientation! Whether you’re a first‑time intern or a returning intern, we’d love a short 10–15 second vertical selfie video from you.
New interns: Answer the question: Why did you choose Caterpillar for your internship? [Sample: Hi, I’m [Name] and I’ll be working in [Role]. I picked Caterpillar for my internship because…]
Returning interns: Answer the question: What is one word or piece of advice you would give to incoming interns? [Sample: Hi, I’m [Name] and one piece of advice I’d give to incoming interns is…]
Start with your name and role/business area, record in a quiet space, and make sure no logos are present unless they’re Caterpillar related
Submit by April 3, 2026 to universityrelations@cat.com<mailto:universityrelations@cat.com>
Subject Line (as appropriate)

  *   Name – Function – New Intern Selfie Video
  *   Name – Function – Returning Intern Selfie Video
We can’t wait to feature you!

[cid:image003.png@01DCB6BB.536E9D50]

IMPORTANT: EMPLOYMENT DOCUMENTS CHECKLIST
As part of your pre-onboarding process, you’ll need to complete Form I-9 (Employment Eligibility Verification). This is a legal requirement for employers to confirm the identity and work authorization of all new employees hired in the United States. We use an automated I-9 verification process through our partner, First Advantage. Watch your inbox (and spam folder) for an email from “First Advantage” with instructions and next steps. As you head into Spring Break, review the attached checklist (A-Notice to Prospective Applicants form) to ensure you have the required documents to complete your I-9 verification. Having everything prepared ahead of time will help avoid delays or issues with your start date.

GROUP CHAT FOR ALL INTERNS
Join our GroupMe chat to connect with interns across Caterpillar—meet new people, find roommates or carpool partners, and connect over shared interests. It’s an easy way to build community beyond your business unit or function. Review the attached GroupMe Rules of Engagement and JOIN HERE!<https://groupme.com/join_group/112859477/tPqV79BQ>
Note: Participation is completely optional.

ACTION ITEMS
If you haven’t already, please complete the following:

  *   Send your transcripts by April 1, including completed Fall 2025 courses and in-progress Spring 2026 courses, with your cumulative GPA.
     *   Unofficial Transcripts are acceptable.
     *   Please send transcripts to Lauren.Southwood@cat.com

STAY UP TO DATE ON ALL THINGS CATERPILLAR
Caterpillar Transforms the Construction Worksite with Advanced Technology, Services and Solutions at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2026<https://www.caterpillar.com/en/news/corporate-press-releases/h/con-expo-2026.html>
Caterpillar Teams With NVIDIA to Revolutionize Heavy Industry with Physical AI and Robotics<https://www.caterpillar.com/en/news/corporate-press-releases/h/cat-nvidia-collab.html>

BEST WISHES FOR A SAFE AND WELL-DESERVED SPRING BREAK!
We can’t wait to see the work you will do to help build a better world!

Facebook<https://www.facebook.com/catcareers/> | LinkedIn<https://www.linkedin.com/company/caterpillar-inc/> | X<https://twitter.com/CaterpillarJobs> | Instagram<https://www.instagram.com/wearecaterpillar/>



Cheers,

Lauren Southwood

subject: Ingram Micro’s First Batch of 2026 Summer Internships Now Open!
body:
[A blue and black logo  Description automatically generated]<https://corp.ingrammicro.com/>



[Feature image alt text copy 600x250]
2026 Summer Internship Program





We’re excited to share that the first batch of Ingram Micro’s 2026 Summer Internship roles is now live!

Review and apply to the openings here: Ingram Micro's 2026 Summer Internship Postings<https://careers.ingrammicro.com/en/jobs/?search=intern&country=United+States+of+America&pagesize=20#results>

This first batch of postings includes opportunities in Marketing, Corporate Communications, Environmental Sustainability, Financial Planning & Analysis, Audit, Business Analysis, Supply Chain Strategy, Marketing, and more, across our Irvine, CA and Buffalo, NY offices.

Stay tuned for a second batch of internships to be posted soon, with a focus on Information Systems, Technology, Digital Innovation, and Data-driven functions.

Summer Internship Program Details:

Ingram Micro’s Summer Internship Program is a 10-week, hybrid, paid opportunity starting in June, offering hands-on experience in Marketing, Finance, Information Systems, and more. Expect immersive learning, professional growth opportunities, and networking events that shape both your personal and professional journey. Explore more details through our Early Career Programs<https://careers.ingrammicro.com/en/locations/north-america/united-states/early-careers/> site.

Important Dates:

• March: Applications open, with postings remaining live for at least 3 days. After that, postings may be removed at any time due to applicant volume. We recommend submitting your application as soon as possible.
• March - April: Recruiters and hiring managers will review applications and reach out to candidates on a rolling basis. Interview timelines may vary by position, but candidates selected for interviews may expect to hear from us by the end of April. We kindly ask that you keep that timing in mind as we work through the process.
• June 1 - August 7: First cohort start & end date
• June 15 - August 21: Second cohort start & end date

We look forward to reviewing your application.

*You are receiving this email because you signed up for Ingram Micro’s Early Career Programs mailing list. If you expressed interest in our Global Platform Technology Fresh Grad Rotation Program (for upcoming Software Engineering/Computer Science-related grads), a separate email will be sent regarding that program later this spring.

subject: New Post Available | Check Out The Latest News
sender: Women in Tech Mentorship New Post <hello@oneuponedown.org>
body:
| ## Inner Circle Masterclass Series: From Execution to Strategic Mindset with Michal Lasman

A Trust-Led Leadership Accelerator for Senior ICs, managers, and leaders moving into broader strategic roles.

View and join the Inner Circle Masterclass Events This IWD season, we're living the theme 'Give To Gain', and that starts with investing in the leaders in our community who are ready for their next chapter.

We've partnered with Executive Coach to bring you a three-part masterclass series designed for women who consistently deliver and are ready to expand their impact beyond execution.

Because the skills that got you here won't always be the ones that take you further.

The series covers:

🧠 Masterclass 1: Strategic & Executional Mindsets,  19 March, 6pm CET

🪞 Masterclass 2: Self-Awareness & Emotional Regulation,  9 April, 6pm CET

🎤 Masterclass 3: Communicate with Impact, 23 April, 6pm CET

Each session is interactive, practical, and grounded in real leadership challenges.

Who This Is For:

  • Senior ICs, managers, and leaders moving into broader strategic roles
  • High performers ready to increase influence and organisational impact
  • Leaders operating in fast-paced, high-pressure environments

Who This Is Not For:

  • Early-career professionals focused primarily on skill building & execution

About The Facilitator:

Michal Lasman is a global GTM and product marketing leader turned executive coach. She's spent her career working with founders, product, and revenue leaders at high-growth tech companies, and now she helps senior leaders move from delivery into strategic influence. She's a certified executive coach, PMA Berlin community leader, and host of the CMO Convo podcast.

View and join the Inner Circle Masterclass Events

subject: Get Ready: Ingram Micro's 2026 Summer Internships Open Next Week!
body:
[A blue and black logo  Description automatically generated]<https://corp.ingrammicro.com/>



[Feature image alt text copy 600x250]
2026 Summer Internship Program




Thank you for your interest in Ingram Micro's Summer Internship Program!

We're excited to announce that we will soon be starting the recruiting process for our 2026 Summer Internship Program. We will be posting the positions for the program next week. Depending on applicant volume, some postings may close after only a few days, so we encourage you to apply as soon as they go live.

Summer Internship Program Details:

Ingram Micro's Summer Internship Program is a 10-week, hybrid, paid opportunity starting in June, offering hands-on experience in Marketing, Finance, Information Systems, and more. Expect immersive learning, professional growth opportunities, and networking events that shape both your personal and professional journey. Explore more details through our Early Career Programs<https://careers.ingrammicro.com/en/locations/north-america/united-states/early-careers/> site.

Who We're Looking For:


  *   Candidates who are actively enrolled in an accredited college/university program, or recently graduated (within the last semester or quarter) from an accredited college/university program. Juniors or seniors are preferred.
  *   Candidates who are able to commit to a summer assignment working onsite at our Irvine, CA or Buffalo, NY offices. Please note, this program does not offer relocation assistance.

What to Do Now:


  *   Ensure Your Resume is Ready: Please update your resume with detailed descriptions of relevant experience and education information, including your expected graduation month/year and major.
  *   Be Ready to Apply Next Week: We'll send you another email sometime next week when the internship positions are posted. Keep an eye on your inbox, as some postings may only be available for a few days. We encourage you to apply as soon as possible so you don't miss the opportunity.
  *   Expect to Hear from Us by end of April: Our recruiters and hiring managers will review applications and reach out to candidates on a rolling basis. Interview timelines may vary by position, but candidates selected for interviews may expect to hear from us by the end of April.

*You are receiving this email because you signed up for Ingram Micro's Early Career Programs mailing list. If you expressed interest in our Global Platform Technology Fresh Grad Rotation Program (for upcoming Software Engineering/Computer Science-related grads), a separate email will be sent regarding that program next month. If you no longer wish to receive notifications like this, please reply to this email with "Unsubscribe".

Sincerely,

The Ingram Micro Recruiting Team

subject: Re: Touchpoint - Internship
body:
Cristina,

I forgot to mention about Lakitu. 😊

Yes, it is referring to the Mario Bros character.  This was the original team under Telematics Data Hub.  If you look, he’s floating on a cloud - and in the game, he hurls objects down from the cloud.  This was the basis for choosing this character!

So, we have Mario Bros themed teams:

  *
Lakitu
  *
Tatanga
  *
Tyfoo

We did, at one point, also have a Bowser team.

Really, it’s meant to be light-hearted and fun.  Other teams have themes as well (Military - Whiskey/Tango/Foxtrot; API Responses - 402, 404).  Then there are teams like “Justateam” which is funny all by itself.  😊

Tina Williams
Software Engineering Manager
Telematics DataHub | Cat Digital | Caterpillar Inc.
Phone: (309)368-0741 (C)
Email: Williams_Tina_M@cat.com<mailto:Williams_Tina_M@cat.com>

Use caution when opening emails and do not click on links or attachments from unknown senders. For more resources, visit security.cat.com/phishing.

Hi Tina,

It's so great to hear from you! I apologize for the delayed response, I had been busy with midterms and hadn't had the chance to check my email.

I'm so excited for the upcoming summer as well, I can't wait to learn and develop my skills with you all. I was not familiar with Lakitu but after a quick Google search (I'm sorry for cheating), I assume you are referring to the little yellow character from Mario? That is such a fun team name, and I'm so curious as to how it came about. I look forward to meeting you all and getting to know both you and Chris before joining the team in May.

I have been assigned to the Chicago office, and wanted to let you know that I unfortunately will not be able to attend orientation in Peoria due to a prior commitment. During our informational meeting, you mentioned you work out of LC and the majority of the team works out of the Chicago office, do you visit the city often? I hope we have the opportunity to meet in person!

Looking forward to our meeting and hearing from you and Chris soon.

Best,
Cristina Le

RE: Caterpillar Internship - Next Steps - CRISTINA LE - File # 3696035
body:
Hi Cristina,

Caterpillar has provided us with new information regarding finding roommates for your housing.  I wanted to pass it along!

Interns interested in shared housing or roommates may choose to explore Switchplace, a housing search and roommate‑matching platform for students and early‑career professionals.


  *   Participation is optional, and all housing decisions and arrangements are the intern’s responsibility.
  *   Use this link to being and access Switchplace - Switchplace<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.switchplace.com/cat-intern-housing?hs_preview=bXuZJhhJ-206768541612__;!!FtR4BK4x7WL3xYs!_nAG0dmbazymQZTZJARB7Bzv8uJm7kTSl6cyxRGRv-JuFZLgZuYPlqv7qmgrVg3Aif0luRigXHNFv3Qlk6MEkw$>

If you prefer to connect with other interns directly, your onboarding contact at Caterpillar can provide guidance on how to reach interns in your area who may also be seeking roommates.

Thank you and please let me know if you have any questions.


Kind regards,
Carolina

From: Yamada, Carolina
Sent: Monday, February 23, 2026 3:25 PM
To: Cristina Le <cristinathoale@gmail.com>
Cc: Cartus: Email Integration Americas <AMERICASemailintegrationsupport@cartus.com>
Subject: Caterpillar Internship - Next Steps - CRISTINA LE - File # 3696035

Hello Cristina,

Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today. It was a pleasure to e-meet you and I look forward to assisting you on this Journey with Caterpillar! 😊

Following our discussion, here are the next steps:

Allowance Payment
You will receive a one-time allowance payment of USD 9,296.50. You will receive an email to submit this for processing two months prior to your start date on May 26th, 2026. Once submitted, it will take a few additional days to process and transfer the funds to your account. Please note that this payment cannot be made if you do not submit the request when you receive the link. Therefore, keep an eye out for the email on March 27th and let me know if you do not receive it.

This payment is intended to assist you with offsetting costs for expenses such as but not limited to:

  *   Housing Allowance:  $ 5,300
  *   Meal & Lodging Allowance: $ 1,140
  *   Transportation Allowance: $ 2,856.50

Please be sure to manage your funds wisely! You will be responsible for arranging your own transportation and housing.

Housing

  *   The housing allowance can be used at your discretion.
  *   You will be responsible for securing your accommodations. You can stay in a dorm, hotel, etc.
  *   DO NOT DELAY setting up your housing. Please see attached Tips for Securing Housing.  Book EARLY if you want to find the best rates and options.
  *   Should you wish to roommate with other interns in your area, please see your FAC at Caterpillar. They can assist you with how to contact other interns that may be interested in the same in your area.
  *   Should you have any issues securing housing, please let me know so we can track for Caterpillar.
  *   Should a hotel be needed, please see attached for a list of Caterpillar negotiated hotel discount codes.
  *   To aid in your search, I’ve attached a list of accommodations available to rent within general area of your internship location. Please note these are provided for your convenience as options to consider, but we recommend that you verify housing independently as no endorsements or guarantees are made.

     *   You will be responsible for contacting the accommodation to confirm availability and securing should you choose to use this option.
     *   We have provided high level information for each accommodation, but please review in more detail to ensure the location, amenities, and policies are acceptable to you.

Please contact me should you have any questions. I will be happy to assist.

Kind regards,
Carolina

subject: Want Your Resume to Stand Out? Add Data & AI Skills 🚀
body:
March 2026 

 Are you ready
  to level up?  

 Hi Cristina, 

	New month, new ways to move your career. Let's turn curiosity into momentum. 

	Peek inside our new brand campaign #LikeanIBMer, explore a 16-week Co‑op program that lets you deliver real work, and see how Parth’s curiosity led him from software to quantum and to IBM. Level up fast with our free SkillsBuild Data and AI sprint for resume ready skills. Finally, rewind to Deep Blue’s win over Garry Kasparov to see how bold tech moments fuel what comes next.   
   [1] 

	As part of our new campaign #LikeanIBMer you'll see many stories revealed of what working at IBM truly means. Watch this video featuring five IBMers in key roles around the world who are driving change in their careers and inspire us. 
 [2] Watch the video 

Can’t miss this 👇?

   [3]    

	First weeks at IBM through Kennedy’s lens with Austin energy and an influencer’s POV. 

	[4] Read it on Instagram   	  [5]    

	A 16-week, full-time on-site co‑op where you can deliver real software or support sales and turn campus skills into impact. 

	[6] Explore the program   	  [7]    

	From Mumbai IT grad to IBM Ireland full-stack dev, Parth’s detour into quantum shows how curiosity can rewrite your path. 

	[8] Read it on Linkedin   

 Your free monthly brain booster ? 

 Note: You'll have to sign into our free SkillsBuild learning platform to access the course below. 

 [9]  Kickstart your Data and AI journeys   

	 Fast-track Data and AI fundamentals this March on our webinars with hands-on modules and stackable credentials. Practical, free, and designed for real career outcomes. Limited spots available. 

	Date: 18th & 25th March, 2026   

  	 [10]     	   

 Around the world 🌍

	Inside Deep Blue: IBM’s Historic Chess Project 

	In 1997, IBM’s Deep Blue did something that no machine had done before. In May of that year, it became the first computer system to defeat reigning world chess champion Garry Kasparov in a match under standard tournament controls. [11]  Read more about Deep Blue   

Update Your Profile!

	Been working for 2+ years? You're no longer Entry Level! 

	Update your profile to get the Professionals Newsletter with content for your career stage. 

	1. Log into your [12] IBM Account.  

	2. Go to the "Your Information" tab and click "Edit your information".  

	3. Select Professional (>3 years) in the "Experience Level" field.  

	4. Save your changes! 

  	 [13]     	   

 📢 Stay ahead: set up job alerts 

	Be the first to know when new roles open that match your skills and interests. 

	 Here’s how to set up your personalized alerts:  

	1. Log in to your [14] IBM account. 

	2. Go to the "Job Alerts" tab and click "Create Job Alert." 

	3. Fill out the form by entering relevant keywords and selecting other preferences to tailor alerts to your interests. 

	4. Click "Submit", and you’re all set! 

	You'll start receiving job alerts to your email based on your chosen frequency.  

 This is the sign you've been looking for!

 [15]  Apply to our open roles   	   

	Keep in touch! 

	The IBM Talent Acquisition Team  

	Did a friend forward this to you? 

	 You can [16] subscribe here.    

	 Connect with us 
 [17]  [18]  [19]  [20]    

 ©IBM Corporation 2026. IBM, the IBM logo, IBM Cloud™ and ibm.com are trademarks of the International Business Machines Corp., registered in many jurisdictions worldwide. This document is current as of the initial date of publication and may be changed by IBM at any time. Not all offerings are available in every country in which IBM operates.

 To ensure delivery to your inbox, please add talent@ibm.com to your address book. Please do not reply to this email, as we are unable to respond from this address.

 If you’d like us to refrain from sending you similar e-mails in the future, please click on the unsubscribe link below. You may also mail a written request to IBM US at: IBM Corporation, 11501 Burnet Rd, Bldg 904 3B-000, Austin, TX 78758.   

This email is being sent on behalf of IBM. To unsubscribe from further emails on this list, please click [21] here.

Rules, policies, and cancellations

ONT

Sat, May 23, 2026 - Sun, May 24, 2026

  • Seat assignments not available until after check-in
  • Refunds and ticket changes are not permitted
  • Same-day confirmed and same-day standby travel changes are not permitted
  • Priority Boarding not available for purchase
  • IMPORTANT - Limitation on Use of Flight Credits If you use a flight credit to book a trip through Chase Travel, any unused portion of the credit will be forfeited, notwithstanding any provisions to the contrary in the airline contract of carriage. No such forfeiture will occur if the credit is used for flight of greater or equal value to the credit.
  • Carry-on baggage restrictions will apply. The selected airline's baggage fees will apply. Click here for details.
  • If this is an international flight, this reservation requires proof of citizenship. Please contact the consulate destination for current VISA/Passport entry requirements.
  • The Transportation Security Administration requires specific information for each passenger such as full name, date of birth and gender to be provided to the airline at least 72 hours prior to departure. If this information was not provided for each passenger at the time of booking, it is the passenger's responsibility to ensure this information is provided to the airline to prevent additional screening, travel delays or denied transport. For more information, refer to TSA's website at TSA.
  • Important Notice Regarding Airline Fees: Airlines may charge additional fees for miscellaneous services including baggage. Fees vary by airline so you must contact the airline directly or check their website for up-to-date information and pricing details.
  • Your personal data will be processed in accordance with the applicable carrier's privacy policy and, if your booking is made via a reservation system provider ("GDS"), with its privacy policy. These are available at https://www.iatatravelcentre.com/privacy.htm or from the carrier or GDS directly. You should read this documentation, which applies to your booking and specifies, for example, how your personal data is collected, stored, used, disclosed and transferred.
  • Please refer to the Terms and Conditions for more information.
  • Cancellations may incur a cancellation fee up to the full amount of the ticket.
  • Changes may incur a change fee up to the full amount of the ticket plus any difference in the applicable fare.
  • This ticket is non-refundable.
  • Some non-refundable tickets can be applied (for a limited time) toward future travel, upon the collection of a change fee up to the full amount of the ticket plus any difference in the applicable fare.
  • Changes to your itinerary are generally permitted upon the collection of a change fee up to the full amount of the ticket plus any difference in the applicable fare. Some tickets do not allow changes.

Questions?

If you need more immediate assistance, call a Chase Travel Specialist at 1-866-331-0773 or +1-206-274-6380 (international number, charges may apply) and have your Trip ID 1014422745 ready.

subject: Re: Form Submission - Contact Form
body:
Hey Cristina,

Thank you for reaching out to us! A few questions to help us find the best
fit clinician for you. Also if you can provide a brief description of the
service you are seeking. (i.e. Anxiety, Depression, PTSD...etc)

   - What insurance will you be using? Please provide Insurance name, your
   Member ID and Date of Birth.
   - What days and times work best for you?  Preference for Morning,
   afternoons or evenings?
   - Are you looking for in person or telehealth sessions?

I look forward to hearing from you soon!

subject: Check your 26-27 FAFSA form off your to-do list!
body:
Don't miss out on student aid for 2026–27. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Click here to view this email as a web page.
https://track.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=ted6b4559,a5f6d22,6863403 Complete Your 2026–27 FAFSA ® Form Today Cristina, The 2026–27 Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA ® ) form is available at https://track.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=ted6b4559,a5f6d22,6863404 fafsa.gov . If you plan to attend college or career/trade school between July 1, 2026, and June 30, 2027, fill out your FAFSA form as soon as possible . State deadlines for financial aid this month are coming up fast, and some aid is only available on a first-come, first-served basis. https://track.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=ted6b4559,a5f6d22,6863405 Start Your FAFSA Remember: You must submit a FAFSA form every year you're in school to stay eligible for federal student aid. FAFSA ® Resources for Students Check your https://track.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=ted6b4559,a5f6d22,6863406 FAFSA deadlines . Your state, your school, and the federal government all have different FAFSA deadlines. Some deadlines are approaching this month: keep track of them and submit your FAFSA form as early as possible. Read our https://track.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=ted6b4559,a5f6d22,6863407 " FAFSA ® Checklist: What Students Need" article to be prepared. You'll learn what you need to gather to access and complete your form. Get https://track.mail.studentaid.gov/r/?id=ted6b4559,a5f6d22,6863408 pro tips for the FAFSA form . Learn key information that will help you fill out your form and avoid issues.  
 

How helpful was this email?
1
2

Not at all
helpful		 Extremely
helpful
 

Need help filling out the FAFSA® form or have questions about your form? Log in to StudentAid.gov to see your form's status, and check out the FAFSA® Help Center for application tips and other useful information.

Sign up for text alerts for important updates on your FAFSA form.

This email was sent by: Office of Federal Student Aid
U.S. Department of Education
400 Maryland Ave SW,
Washington, DC 20002

 

Please do not reply to this email. Messages sent to this email address are not monitored. If you wish to contact us, please use the StudentAid.gov contact page. For more information about financial aid, visit StudentAid.gov. If you do not want to receive future FSA informational emails, unsubscribe.

subject: Re: New client inquiry from your Professional Website
body:
Hi Cristina,

No need to apologize and I was about to email you as well. It looks like
you have a $3,400 deductible. What this means is, until you spend that
amount, co-insurance doesn't pick up (which is 20%). It may be helpful to
ask your parent if this is a family deductible or just your deductible. I
recognize this is a significant upfront cost, so I understand your decision
to cancel our consultation appointment. It may be helpful to ask your
parent and/or call the insurance company directly (using the phone number
on your insurance card) to find out if the deductible applies to everything
or just medical expenses. For some plans, the deductible does not apply to
mental health visits. I hope this helps! Below is what I see on my
provider's end.

Best,


Dr. La


Insurance coverage
Last verified on 3/3
Current estimated responsibility
100.00% Co-Insurance

20% Co-Insurance
Out of Pocket Max - $6,000.00
$33.57 spent
$5,966.43 remaining
Deductible - $3,400.00

subject: You have in-network rates with Amy
body:
You can see Amy L. in-network

We estimate you'll pay $170-$210* per session

Good news! Alma has confirmed your active insurance coverage for your care with Amy.

We estimate you'll pay $170-$210* per session.

Our estimation is based on the insurance information you provided to us. Please let Alma or your provider know if your insurance changes in the future.

Sign in to review your results

Next up: Amy L. will book an appointment with you.

Now that Alma has verified your insurance benefits, Amy L. will reach out to you to book an appointment.

Amy L. will submit a claim to your insurance company after each appointment. You can expect to be charged by Alma once your insurance has processed your claim, which can take up to 60 days.

*About your estimate

Your payment responsibility is an estimate provided by your insurance plan. This estimate reflects your insurance coverage eligibility as of March 4, 2026. Your responsibility is subject to change based on your insurance company’s review of the services rendered by your provider. If you have any questions, please send us a support request.

Need a crash course on insurance?

It can be difficult to understand how health insurance works and which services are covered. We created a simple guide that walks you through the basics of insurance, including definitions of key terms you may see in the future. Read our insurance guide.

Alma support
Copyright © 2026 Alma • Privacy Policy

subject: Get ready for your appointment with Amy La
body:
Welcome to Amy La's practice

Hi Cristina,

Amy has sent you some documents to fill out through Alma, the platform they've partnered with to help manage their practice.

Create an account to securely complete your documents:

Create account

This is a one-time link that is personal to you. Do not forward it to anyone else.

Complete the documents before your first appointment to make sure Amy has the information they need to get you set up with their practice.

Insurance and Payment

Amy needs this information to make sure you’re billed correctly for your sessions.

  • Insurance details
  • Autopay enrollment • Required by Alma

Intake Forms

These forms provide important information about Amy’s policies and procedures.

Create account

Alma support

You received this email because your provider is a member of Alma, a community of mental health practitioners. Alma occasionally sends emails on your provider’s behalf and always keeps your information secure.

body:
Hi Cristina,

The 15-minute consultation is free. Do you want me to run your insurance
before our consultation? There's no obligation even if I do that. I use
Alma for insurance billing so if you receive an email from them, it's not
spam. It will tell me if we are in-network and what your financial
responsibility will be (e.g., copay or coinsurance). I'll schedule a video
consultation for this Thursday at 4 PM. Here's the link as well:
https://video.simplepractice.com/appt-23e55599bb0ddc12a0529a5d0f61d3f9
If anything comes up, then just let me know.

Best,


Dr. La

Dear Cristina,

This month in the OpenAI Forum, we’re bringing together people from across science, journalism, sports, health, and culture to explore how AI is showing up in the real world — not just as an idea, but as something people are actively building with today.

From a global research convening with UCLA’s Institute for Pure and Applied Mathematics on how frontier reasoning models are accelerating scientific discovery, to conversations about how AI is being used in newsrooms, fan experiences, and personal health journeys, March’s lineup spans a wide range of perspectives and real-world applications. We’ll also replay our recent Forum gathering in India where we explored how AI can expand access to cultural heritage. Whether you join us virtually or in person, we hope these sessions spark new ideas, thoughtful discussion, and a deeper look at how AI is shaping the future across communities.

Interested in telling your story in the Forum? We’re collecting real-world stories from Forum members about how you’re using ChatGPT responsibly in everyday life, work, and research. Your experiences help us understand what’s useful, what others want to learn more about, and how we can continue to deliver relevant conversations and programming for the Forum community. 👉Share your story here. (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/VVH1dS5Kysx2W4q0JJh8HSjwqW5DT7xy5L7M0SN39GZCv3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3m-W4XLHTJ1Ytxh0N7CyksK_btGnVpz-G18-HCyBW33v_Qq9cN8GVW8MbV8X8bywcNW1MV_7l5bMGR9W43PvRk20RdGVW6LbTk-3GLbq0W2c2ts51k9gp6W9kyxML5v18PTW72FCyN1KY0Y-W6gXHX694CR5VW6r6Ktl3YGXKVW54-YDg5FYTvSW2ZytJ67Vy_t0W2hS3gG2w67qZW2Kd2xt52F3kPW1k1rkj8Yxdb8N5KWtrQ-pHlJW4Tdm5w3BpkjHN4nzSbD52WVBW6g4M4C2mY8PWf5Q6PtY04 )

Caitlin Maltbie

Community Manager, Member of Global Affairs Staff, OpenAI

Upcoming Community Events

Wednesday, March 4 @ 11:50am PST

Virtual Event: OpenAI is collaborating with UCLA’s Institute for Pure and Applied Mathematics (IPAM) on a full-day convening of leading mathematicians, theoretical physicists, and AI researchers to explore how frontier reasoning models are accelerating discovery in highly abstract scientific domains. Through lectures, disciplinary deep dives, and panel discussions, the program will spotlight emerging applications of AI and the shifting frontier of progress on some of science’s hardest problems. Midway through the day, a fireside conversation between Terence Tao and Mark Chen revisits last year’s landmark dialogue on AI and mathematical discovery, reflecting on the past year of advances and what comes next. Anyone in the world can watch two featured discussions via the OpenAI Forum: the Tao–Chen fireside chat (moderated by James Donovan) and an AI and Physics panel led by Zvi Bern with Eva Silverstein, Alex Lupasca, Wahid Bhimji, and Kyle Cranmer.

Thursday, March 12 @ 8:00am EDT

In-Person Event: AI is increasingly part of how journalists research, analyze, and tell stories. In this Forum conversation, journalists, editors, and technologists come together to talk about how AI is being used in news organizations today, what responsible integration looks like in practice, and how guidance and shared learnings can help ensure these tools strengthen high-quality journalism and serve the public interest. Immediately following the Forum talk, an OpenAI Academy workshop will feature concrete demos and workflows from Scott Smallwood, founder of Open Campus, a nonprofit newsroom covering higher education and from Evan Hirsch, who led AI integration at NBC News Now.

Saturday, March 14 @ 4:00pm CT

In Person Event: This session is part of SXSW programming, and a valid SXSW badge is required for entry. How do you scale AI without losing the human touch? In this session, leaders from the San Antonio Spurs and OpenAI share how they’re using AI to create more meaningful experiences for fans, partners, and communities. From personalized fan engagement and family-focused experiences to internal productivity and decision-making, the Spurs offer a real-world look at how purpose-built AI tools are being used on and off the court. Attendees will walk away with practical lessons on responsible AI adoption, concrete use cases from a global sports franchise, and a blueprint for putting people first while bringing cutting-edge technology to the courtside.

Wednesday, March 18 @ 2:00pm PST

Virtual: When GitLab co-founder Sid Sijbrandij was diagnosed with bone cancer, he brought his engineering mindset to the fight. After almost two years of exploring everything that standard medicine could offer, Sid had reached the end of what doctors could offer him. Sid used ChatGPT to help track and understand his cancer and health data. He inputted scans, blood tests, and tissue samples to build a personal research and development loop that let him connect the dots, spot changes, and explore new potential treatments alongside his medical team. The result helped him move faster, ask sharper questions, and coordinate care across a complex landscape. In this OpenAI Forum talk, Sid and geneticist Jacob Stern share what it means to turn AI into a partner in health research.

Tuesday, March 24 @ 8:00am PST, 9:30pm ISJ

Virtual: This session is a replay of a recorded OpenAI Forum event originally held in India. The program brought together OpenAI, Ask Mona, and the Sanskriti Foundation—one of India’s leading cultural institutions—to explore how AI can transform the way people experience museums. Hosted at Travancore Palace in New Delhi, the event featured an exhibition of ancient terracotta sculptures from the Sanskriti Museum, where visitors scanned QR codes to engage with a multilingual AI-powered docent and ask questions in real time. Rather than replacing curators or scholars, the experience introduced a new layer of interpretation—helping visitors follow their curiosity and connect with cultural heritage in a more personal and accessible way. The program concluded with a panel discussion featuring Ronnie Chatterji, Marion Carré, Varun Jain, and additional guests.

About the OpenAI Forum

The OpenAI Forum is a vibrant global community of experts committed to advancing OpenAI's mission: building artificial general intelligence (AGI) that benefits everyone.

Since 2023, the OpenAI Forum has been building an ecosystem of collaborators with the intention of forging impactful partnerships, exchanging resources and ideas, and amplifying AI use cases working to solve humanity's most challenging problems.

What to Expect:

The Forum offers regular virtual and in-person programming—including expert panels, OpenAI-led research talks, ChatGPT tutorials, networking events, and dinner mixers at OpenAI offices. You’ll meet trailblazers across disciplines: from astronomers and neuroscientists to artists, public sector leaders, economists, and more. You’re not just an attendee—you’re a welcomed guest at every event.

Network of Experts:

Meet and collaborate with the people operating at the highest expression of expertise in their field.

Public Resources & Replays: Anyone in the world is welcome to join the OpenAI Forum as a guest. Guests can attend live streamed events and explore and share our growing archive of past talks, essays, and learning resources.

The community is composed of 2 tiers of engagement:

- Guests: All you have to do is sign up and activate your profile by validating your email.
- Members: in order to become a full member of the OpenAI Forum, you need to

- Complete your profile, including a headshot, and a short bio section.
- Demonstrate expertise or significant level of experience in your discipline, or area of practice.
- Register for an upcoming event.

Who Qualifies as an Expert?

We define expertise as deep insight in a specific area—gained through study, practice, creative work, lived experience, or leadership. We welcome individuals shaping their fields through research, storytelling, policy, entrepreneurship, education, or advocacy. Credentials matter, but so do grassroots leadership, interdisciplinary work, and nontraditional paths. Expertise comes in many forms, across identities, geographies, and disciplines.

Instructions to update your profile and be considered for full membership:

- Login to forum.openai.com (https://dxBJLx04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dxBJLx04/VVH1dS5Kysx2W4q0JJh8HSjwqW5DT7xy5L7M0SN39GZCv3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3m1W5JHvD938xgk_N1sx0WfGgN7sW41Dg_78wsP-SN6qwqQ97p3J0W43MCJc1bGsqWW4D1ydd3WjmTNW8B2bH-6GQkbdW1m7q257FPKtZW5_LbKt7fWGxcN1M3RlxydyP7W9byHrL91Wx17W3NXJqQ4VGrnDW3z71Qx8GGkHrW2tKh_J6M1Bg9MVtswyyhhfcMJRyQb5xJ2xW84rLpR5VkMZSW8Ldl4j4d3xM3W1SYcG52gx-W1W5hG8qB4sQX4kW1QLSLZ1cQ6q_W1Q3tYC1vDH2zf8LfbXv04 ) with the email from which you are receiving this newsletter
- Access the magic link in your inbox.
- Once you're on the platform, in the top right corner click your profile picture or username.
- Select "Edit Profile."
- Add your headshot, update your bio, location and details about your interests and expertise.
- Save your changes. If you have any questions, please email forum-support@openai.com.

Southwest LUV Vouchers are valid as a form of payment toward future airfare only on Southwest Airlines through Southwest.com®, on the Southwest Airlines app and mobile website (only for initial flight booking), toll-free Reservations 1‑800‑I‑FLY‑SWA (1-800-435-9792), or at any Southwest Airlines ticket counter.

2

Southwest LUV Vouchers must be redeemed and all travel completed by the expiration date indicated. If applied in conjunction with any other accepted form(s) of payment, the earliest expiration date of all payments applies. Once the voucher is applied to a reservation, the funds can only be used by the ticketed Passenger. Validity of Southwest LUV Vouchers will not be extended, and any unused value will be forfeited upon expiration.

3

Southwest LUV Vouchers are not redeemable as payment toward government-imposed segment fees, excise taxes, Passenger Facility Charges, or September 11th Security Fees or as payment toward optional travel charges, Southwest Airlines group tickets, Getaways by Southwest™ vacation bookings, or travel either wholly or in part on other air carriers.

4

Southwest LUV Vouchers and travel issued in exchange for Southwest LUV Vouchers have no cash value and cannot be exchanged for cash or other monetary consideration. Southwest LUV Vouchers will not be replaced if lost, stolen, or destroyed.

5

To redeem a Southwest LUV Voucher, the purchaser must have a Southwest LUV Voucher number and associated security code. If a balance remains after redemption, retain the Southwest LUV Voucher number and security code for future use.

6

Southwest LUV Vouchers are VOID if sold, altered, purchased, brokered, and/or bartered.

View remaining funds and status of your Southwest LUV Voucher at Southwest.com or call 1-866-393-2081.

subject: Your March 1 Flight (F1980 IND AUS)
body:
[cid:image001.jpg@01DCA9DD.11E79B10]<http://www.southwest.com/>
March 2, 2026

Dear Cristina,

On behalf of all of us at Southwest Airlines, please accept our sincere apologies for the delay you experienced on Flight #1980 in Indianapolis, IN (IND). We know how critical timely travel is, and we regret that Crew scheduling challenges caused a significant disruption to your plans. I hope you will accept my sincere apology for any inconvenience caused by our tardy arrival to Austin.

We are a Company built on serving others through the delivery of safe and reliable air transportation. Your continued patronage is important to us, and we hope you continue giving us the privilege of seeing you onboard.  In that spirit, I'm sending you a LUV Voucher (in a separate e-mail, which will arrive within fourteen days) that can be applied as a form of payment toward a new Southwest reservation.  We look forward to welcoming you onboard another flight in the near future.

Kind regards,

Jhericca

Jhericca

2702 Love Field Drive
Dallas, TX 75235

Choice Extra, Choice Preferred, and Choice Transferable Flight Credits created from reservations booked and ticketed and/or changed on or after May 28, 2025 expire twelve months from the date the fare was purchased and ticketed. Basic fare flight credits expire six months from the date the fare was purchased and ticketed.

Prohibition on Multiple/Conflicting Reservations: To promote seat availability for our Customers, Southwest® prohibits multiple reservations for the same Passenger departing from the same city on the same date, or any multiple reservations containing conflicting or overlapping itineraries (such as departures for the same Customer from multiple cities at the same time). Furthermore, without advance notice to the Passenger or purchaser, Southwest may cancel such reservations, or any other reservations that it believes, in its sole discretion, were made without intent to travel. With the exception of Southwest gift cards, funds from proactively canceled reservations by Southwest will be returned to the original form of payment. Reservations paid for with a Southwest gift card will have the amount applied from the gift card held as a flight credit for use by the Customer on a future Southwest Airlines® flight.

If you have purchased a refundable fare and choose not to travel, you must request your refund to the original payment form within 1 year of ticket issuance.

subject: Changes to your Southwest trip
body:
Confirmation #: AFLDKT

Hello from Southwest Airlines®,

You have been rebooked to LGB on Southwest Airlines® #998 departing IND at 5:15 AM on March 2. Your connection is Southwest Airlines® Flight #3048 departing PHX at 8:50 AM on March 2. This change was made due to a disruption to your original itinerary. We apologize for any inconvenience this causes you.

If you have questions about your flight, we encourage you to view your new itinerary (https://link.swa.info/4aXysx9190b).

If your new itinerary doesn't work, you can change your seat (https://link.swa.info/UOCxUvluMVb) or you can rebook your flight (https://link.swa.info/N2Vv3VruMVb) up to two times with confirmation number (). 

If you decide not to travel, you are eligible for a refund. See details (https://support.southwest.com/helpcenter/s/pathway/a1F5G00000cLOqqUAG/cancellations-oe-significant-delays?RMID=AOM Cancel&src=MAILAOMCANCEL) about your available options. Your reservation must be canceled at least 10 minutes before the new flight's scheduled departure time.

Thank you for flying with us. We'll see you onboard soon.

The Southwest® Team

3 Eligible Participant: Open only to individuals who (1) have an
existing U.S. Venmo account (“Valid Account”), (2) are residents
of any one (1) of the fifty (50) United States or the District of
Columbia, (3) are eighteen (18) years of age or older; (4) apply
and are approved for the Venmo Mastercard® ("Card") during the
Enrollment Period (defined below) (eligibility for/those who
receive the Rewards will be determined solely by Venmo)
(“Eligible Participant”).

“Eligible Purchase(s)”: Eligible purchase is defined as in-store
purchases made at eligible campus bookstores (offer is not
available online) using the Card and finalized by the merchant
during the Offer Period (defined below). Eligible Purchases do
not include: (1) purchases that are marked as “pending“ in your
Valid account as of the end of the Offer Period, (2) wire
transfers or cash transfers or any transaction under MCC code
4829 (defined below) (3) ATM transactions, (4) gift card
purchases, (5) any purchase or portion of a purchase that
includes any payment method other than the Card, (6) manual cash
disbursements from a customer financial institution as defined
under MCC code 6010, (7) merchandise purchases at a Card
financial institution location as defined under MCC code 6012,
(8) quasi-cash transactions including travelers checks, money
orders or cryptocurrency transactions, as defined under MCC code
6051, (9) purchases using the Card at novelty or souvenir shops
as defined under MCC code 5947 or, (10) in-store cash
withdrawals/cash back. Merchant Category Code (“MCC”) is assigned
by each merchant, their processor, and the credit card networks.
Venmo is not responsible for assignment of MCC codes.

Enrollment Period: Begins on January 5, 2026, at 12:00:00 a.m.
Pacific Time (“PT”) and ends May 31, 2026, at 11:59:59 p.m. PT.
(“Enrollment Period”).

Offer Period: Offer begins on January 5, 2026, at 11:59:00 p.m.
Pacific Time (“PT”) and ends the earlier of May 31, 2026, at
11:59:59 p.m. PT, or until we reach the max of $375,000 in
available rewards, whichever comes first (“Offer Period”).

“How to Earn”: After activating the Barnes & Noble College offer
by saving it in the Venmo app, Eligible Participants can earn 10%
cash back throughout the Offer Period (up to a maximum of $50
USD) on Eligible Purchases (defined above) using the Card
(“Reward”). The Reward will be added to your Venmo account
associated with the Card that qualified for the Reward and will
be paid within ninety (90) days after the Reward is earned but
will often be available sooner. There is a maximum of $375,000 in
Rewards available under this offer; Rewards will no longer be
available once the limit is reached.

Miscellaneous: Valid Account must remain in good standing from
the time of participation through Reward fulfillment to redeem a
Reward, as determined in Venmo’s sole discretion. Venmo may
provide an alternate reward of equal value if it is unable for
any reason to fulfill the Reward. Venmo reserves the right to
cancel, suspend or modify this offer in part or in its entirety
at any time without notice, for any reason in its sole
discretion. Similar offers may run at the same time;
qualification for this offer does not constitute qualification
for any other offer. Venmo is not responsible and/or liable if
any e-mail, Reward, Eligible Purchases, or offer-related
materials or correspondence are lost, fraudulent, abusive,
stolen, late, incomplete, illegible, interrupted, delayed,
altered, defective, misdirected, tampered with, or irregular in
any way or if any participant’s e-mail address, Valid Account, or
other contact information does not work, is deleted, or is
changed without participant giving prior written notice to Venmo.
Venmo reserves the right to review any account or transaction
related to this offer, in its sole discretion, without notice,
and delay or reverse completion of the Reward. Offer is void
where prohibited and if Eligible Purchases are fraudulent,
abusive, not completed through legitimate channels, or irregular

subject: From candidate to changemaker: Meet Susan
What you'll need for your Indianapolis trip (AFLDKT).
body:
When to arrive, saving time at the airport, & more.

View in Browser

Manage Flight | Flight Status | My Account

The moment you've been waiting for is...almost here. You can check in for your Indianapolis flight at 6:55 AM.

New boarding process: Your flight with assigned seating includes a new boarding process. Learn how it works here.

Do you have a REAL ID? Passengers 18+ need a state-issued REAL ID-compliant license or identification card to fly domestically. Starting February 1, 2026, Passengers who do not have their REAL ID or another TSA acceptable form of ID can pay a $45 fee to use TSA ConfirmID as an alternative identity verification option. Learn more at www.tsa.gov/tsaconfirm-id.

Please allow for extra time as additional screening may cause delays at TSA checkpoints.

Excited for your trip? Here's everything you'll need before you go:

Wanna save time at the airport? Get our app! It can get you a mobile boarding pass, find your gate, give flight updates, and more.

The airport lobby can be a busy place. Make sure you’re ready and show up early. Please arrive at least two hours before your flight departs to check in and go through TSA.

If you use a portable charger/power bank during your flight, keep it out of your bag and in plain sight. Do not charge devices in the overhead bin. Learn more.

With the Basic fare, you get one standard carryon and one personal item per ticketed Passenger. Flight changes allowed with fare upgrade. Learn more.

Get an earlier boarding position with Priority Boarding1! Available starting 24 hours prior to departure. Be in the first group to board and get earlier access to overhead bin space! Buy now.

1Subject to availability.

How to kick back on your day-of travel

Checking a bag? Add your bag(s) and pay any applicable bag fees digitally before arriving at the airport after you check in. Carryon? Carry on to TSA.

 

With Southwest's digital bag tracking, you can track your bag from tagging to arriving at your destination. Learn more about digital bag tracking.

Calories on a plane don't count so have a little treat. You may be able to buy select alcoholic drinks2 on your flight tomorrow. Complimentary water, sodas, coffee, juice, and a snack may also be available.

You’ll get free WiFi3 for Rapid Rewards® Members thanks to T-Mobile®, and free movies3 and TV3 – just bring your own device and headphones.

Learn more about Southwest inflight entertainment here.

Require disability-related assistance while traveling? Visit our Help Center to learn more about requesting accommodations.

Questions?

Check out our Help Center page!

subject: Caterpillar Internship - Next Steps - CRISTINA LE - File # 3696035
body:
Hello Cristina,

Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today. It was a pleasure to e-meet you and I look forward to assisting you on this Journey with Caterpillar! 😊

Following our discussion, here are the next steps:

Allowance Payment
You will receive a one-time allowance payment of USD 9,296.50. You will receive an email to submit this for processing two months prior to your start date on May 26th, 2026. Once submitted, it will take a few additional days to process and transfer the funds to your account. Please note that this payment cannot be made if you do not submit the request when you receive the link. Therefore, keep an eye out for the email on March 27th and let me know if you do not receive it.

This payment is intended to assist you with offsetting costs for expenses such as but not limited to:

  *   Housing Allowance:  $ 5,300
  *   Meal & Lodging Allowance: $ 1,140
  *   Transportation Allowance: $ 2,856.50

Please be sure to manage your funds wisely! You will be responsible for arranging your own transportation and housing.

Housing

  *   The housing allowance can be used at your discretion.
  *   You will be responsible for securing your accommodations. You can stay in a dorm, hotel, etc.
  *   DO NOT DELAY setting up your housing. Please see attached Tips for Securing Housing.  Book EARLY if you want to find the best rates and options.
  *   Should you wish to roommate with other interns in your area, please see your FAC at Caterpillar. They can assist you with how to contact other interns that may be interested in the same in your area.
  *   Should you have any issues securing housing, please let me know so we can track for Caterpillar.
  *   Should a hotel be needed, please see attached for a list of Caterpillar negotiated hotel discount codes.
  *   To aid in your search, I’ve attached a list of accommodations available to rent within general area of your internship location. Please note these are provided for your convenience as options to consider, but we recommend that you verify housing independently as no endorsements or guarantees are made.

     *   You will be responsible for contacting the accommodation to confirm availability and securing should you choose to use this option.
     *   We have provided high level information for each accommodation, but please review in more detail to ensure the location, amenities, and policies are acceptable to you.

Please contact me should you have any questions. I will be happy to assist.

Kind regards,
Carolina

________________________________

Eligible Participant: Open only to individuals who (1) have an

“Eligible Purchase(s)”: Eligible purchase is defined as in-store

4829 (defined below) (3) ATM transactions, (4) gift card

associated with the Card that qualified for the Reward and will
be paid within ninety (90) days after the Reward is earned but
will often be available sooner. There is a maximum of $375,000 in

available once the limit is reached.

subject: Caterpillar Intern Newsletter: February 2026
body:
[cid:image001.png@01DCA0DA.B5F96F50]


BUILDING BEYOND
Caterpillar is changing the way the future is built. For more than a century, we've built a better, more sustainable world. As interns, you're an important part of what comes next. This experience is about building real skills, making connections and contributing ideas that matter. During your internship, you'll face challenges, explore opportunities, and make an impact. You'll grow professionally, and we'll learn from you along the way.

Below are some key milestones to expect as you get closer to your start date:

[cid:image002.jpg@01DCA0DA.B5F96F50]

SECURE YOUR HOUSING
Caterpillar offers relocation benefits to help offset housing costs and support smart financial decisions. Check the attached relocation roadmap for an overview of the process and tips to get started.
Our relocation partner, Cartus, will contact you after you've completed your pre-employment steps (background check, drug screen, etc.). They are reaching out to interns by start date, so thank you for your patience.
Get a Head Start. You don't need to wait for Cartus to begin your search:

  *   Research early: Compare cost, availability, location, amenities, and short-term housing options. Cartus recommendations are a starting point-not a full list.
  *   Secure housing ASAP: Availability is limited, and prices may increase closer to your start date. Your offer letter can serve as proof of employment if needed.
Connect with your Cartus consultant if you have questions or run into housing availability issues.

GET 'ORIENTED' TO CATERPILLAR
We can't wait to kick off our summer internship program with our 2026 Corporate Intern Orientation on Tuesday, May 19. More details are coming soon. In the meantime, check out highlights from the 2025 Corporate Intern Orientation<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6GF_sFUNdIA> to see what to expect.

IMPORTANT: EMPLOYMENT DOCUMENTS CHECKLIST
As part of your pre-onboarding process, you'll need to complete Form I-9 (Employment Eligibility Verification). This is a legal requirement for employers to confirm the identity and work authorization of all new employees hired in the United States. We use an automated I-9 verification process through our partner, First Advantage. Watch your inbox (and spam folder) for an email from "First Advantage" with instructions and next steps. As you head into Spring Break, review the attached checklist (A-Notice to Prospective Applicants form) to ensure you have the required documents to complete your I-9 verification. Having everything prepared ahead of time will help avoid delays or issues with your start date.

GROUP CHAT FOR ALL INTERNS
Join our GroupMe chat to connect with interns across Caterpillar-meet new people, find roommates, or carpool partners, and connect over shared interests. It's an easy way to build community beyond your business unit or function. Review the attached GroupMe Rules of Engagement and JOIN HERE!<https://groupme.com/join_group/112859477/tPqV79BQ>
Note: Participation is completely optional.

ACTION ITEMS
If you haven't already, please complete the following:

  *   Update your personal information in Workday (new employees only), using the instructions sent by Workday.
  *   Please send your updated transcripts to Lauren Southwood by April 1, including completed Fall 2025 courses and in-progress Spring 2026 courses, with your cumulative GPA.

STAY UP TO DATE ON ALL THINGS CATERPILLAR
Caterpillar's Next Chapter Begins at CES 2026<https://www.caterpillar.com/en/ces.html>
Caterpillar CES 2026 Keynote | Building the Future with AI, Autonomy, and Innovation<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vI6k_mJeYU8&utm_source=bing.com&utm_medium=organic&utm_campaign=(not%20set)>
Caterpillar Unveils AI-Powered Future and Invests in the Workforce Building It<https://www.caterpillar.com/en/news/corporate-press-releases/h/ai-powered-future.html>

BEST WISHES FOR A SAFE AND WELL-DESERVED SPRING BREAK!
We can't wait to see the work you will do to help build a better world!

Stay connected with us! Tag us using #iAmCaterpillar
Facebook<https://www.facebook.com/catcareers/> | LinkedIn<https://www.linkedin.com/company/caterpillar-inc/> | X<https://twitter.com/CaterpillarJobs> | Instagram<https://www.instagram.com/wearecaterpillar/>



Cheers,

Lauren Southwood
Digital Talent Acquisition Partner
Caterpillar Inc. | Digital, Technology, and Distribution
Office: 309.578.6574 | Mobile: 309.357.9155 | Email: lauren.southwood@cat.com<mailto:lauren.southwood@cat.com>
StrengthsFinder34 Signature Themes
Relator | Command | Achiever | Futuristic | Adaptability
Connect with me on LinkedIn<https://www.linkedin.com/in/laurensouthwood/>

**My work hours may not be yours. Please do not feel obligated to respond outside of your normal work hours**




Caterpillar: Confidential Green

subject: February Career News from NAVSEA
body:
Employee Spotlight: Carleen Kaye Strengthens NAVSEA’s Workforce and Future Talent
Meet Carleen Kaye, an HR Policy Specialist who has supported NAVSEA’s mission for nearly four years by driving enterprise-wide HR initiatives and leading impactful programs like the DoD SkillBridge, which creates a strong talent pipeline for transitioning service members. Drawn to NAVSEA’s meaningful mission and collaborative, family-oriented culture, Carleen has grown her career while helping streamline processes and strengthen the workforce across commands. Read more about Carleen’s journey and impact on NAVSEA - https://u3445751.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.dRmKBBepCL5WdMYvln7I9a1le3KVGwk-2BVlPHDHmbHXATaG2tei3XWF-2BNjBiDSUxCOSyRLZDb4Wh9OqVF2CVqx2z-2FzgsXSD6akrxrRsMAc34AVWJCt0GahtP2gJkCApDjt1wz4rcvPC5FQHIvITDKSX25oq7OsJm6k-2BfeEpVPcYoHKQef1xLdGYIWyAIjb-2F2MNY-2B1ckzlu63btIwzWI29J-2BNsC-2By5DdMFYMQQ-2BgTsYD7NGLYav-2Fb0uNOl6U7LxaQJA2JNZqKiw9dW7DRPlnjl-2F-2FELwYV3zs2-2FwS0kVQG6FyA-3Dx1KD_wC5ozFR3fdJxOIYV8k7pfvLhj7Y4IzIFYHx6APv06-2Fj5awj7y7UZ-2FYUfo7mxh7pjCz4ynHspPMyNhNqik3rYryKRlJN7mi8b61BBMwwB91Ui7yzAHJGlEOB6wzQ7-2FNeRp2-2BTaO1CCRzl327T4wL2Qg0qwA-2FSMYCBjdSU8ReNePx6kr-2BIOsbwgIZU-2B8NQDA-2FidvVaRH07DyDHCPLlId5miU7SkQ3CYaBnhQnNkK2ZZbXdkUF9PLmxFG1RR0iFpBsKtjK42p0yqCVxXMzo4ZSXtC-2FO5kF3CD6rsX1nm5LNiSKvr58hgQJsej-2BhG1Hfndf76nL0HGRdmYzH-2Ffi3V-2F6TomG99skdFvgWohPVN4LAZUClqpI-2B-2FwdiihQDP9R8r50HvyOd93nGQP1-2BZMol49uzaQ-3D-3D .
 
Set Sail with NAVSEA at the UMD Spring Career Fair!
NAVSEA is excited to attend the University of Maryland Spring Career Fair - https://u3445751.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.dRmKBBepCL5WdMYvln7I9SasirKxoIihG65ZQXYZJKZUNvEsgSiFgFwF7NZgwT-2BbRwRgpHVZ6Xra-2BwZSFQX1XJtnJYm0-2B5IJlvEJW3ZBMM72i2zR4yq0vnHzRLgXIgHCkOJ0DyYURTB4s5qs7umlFA-3D-3DBdmi_wC5ozFR3fdJxOIYV8k7pfvLhj7Y4IzIFYHx6APv06-2Fj5awj7y7UZ-2FYUfo7mxh7pjCz4ynHspPMyNhNqik3rYryKRlJN7mi8b61BBMwwB91Ui7yzAHJGlEOB6wzQ7-2FNeRp2-2BTaO1CCRzl327T4wL2Qg0qwA-2FSMYCBjdSU8ReNePx6kr-2BIOsbwgIZU-2B8NQDA-2FidvVaRH07DyDHCPLlId5miU7SkQ3CYaBnhQnNkK2ZZbXdkUF9PLmxFG1RR0iFpBsKV244cqezOKUYZHCW-2FtoPoqO8xcnbjDOhOGiY8FpR6yjXG0ktIti6DIBcGd-2BeQXrmRn6LIhpuu9hpsb7lycEtXb796TMYmcIzvJy9c-2BwE1YgyhBcj7FRLY4YLjIoNDXx7UX97JmYQ-2FuyggFbzYkmArQ-3D-3D  taking place February 24-26 at the Hotel at UMD. Our team will be on site to connect with talented students and share information about career opportunities supporting the U.S. Navy’s cutting-edge ships, submarines, and systems. Stop by to learn how you can launch your career with meaningful, mission-driven work at NAVSEA.
 
Can’t make it to UMD? Visit the NAVSEA Recruiting Events - https://u3445751.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.dRmKBBepCL5WdMYvln7I9Wi32d2LzKHdZlz84diG5cBO7n1bX6z3jQ2jEQV0Ac0HXZIbDQqi1PIG9-2B0AR46p6g-3D-3D6fXG_wC5ozFR3fdJxOIYV8k7pfvLhj7Y4IzIFYHx6APv06-2Fj5awj7y7UZ-2FYUfo7mxh7pjCz4ynHspPMyNhNqik3rYryKRlJN7mi8b61BBMwwB91Ui7yzAHJGlEOB6wzQ7-2FNeRp2-2BTaO1CCRzl327T4wL2Qg0qwA-2FSMYCBjdSU8ReNePx6kr-2BIOsbwgIZU-2B8NQDA-2FidvVaRH07DyDHCPLlId5miU7SkQ3CYaBnhQnNkK2ZZbXdkUF9PLmxFG1RR0iFpBsK2qH6LLBig1XOYtOPALL66OI8MLfNF37YQUNBYr9mVP8ldsYolUJm3dtS7NaKjoXIkt25py9g5X4vUCRlJgcPwJtg6uIz7O-2B1aY4BfC37ON2n3klq5hC5zjk2PShYylQp-2FGKmgX2h3J69S6xzknAXNA-3D-3D  page to explore other spring recruiting events and find out where we’ll be next!
 
Find a Career You’ll Love
Fall in love with a career that makes a difference this February. Explore NAVSEA’s featured job openings and take the next step toward a mission-driven career, apply today.


Unmanned Surface Vessel Lead Mission Autonomy Software Developer - https://u3445751.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.dRmKBBepCL5WdMYvln7I9edxa57dOCZ5faCLZWyLNFMWUFbw0JldKbnbObcaiMSrYjUU_wC5ozFR3fdJxOIYV8k7pfvLhj7Y4IzIFYHx6APv06-2Fj5awj7y7UZ-2FYUfo7mxh7pjCz4ynHspPMyNhNqik3rYryKRlJN7mi8b61BBMwwB91Ui7yzAHJGlEOB6wzQ7-2FNeRp2-2BTaO1CCRzl327T4wL2Qg0qwA-2FSMYCBjdSU8ReNePx6kr-2BIOsbwgIZU-2B8NQDA-2FidvVaRH07DyDHCPLlId5miU7SkQ3CYaBnhQnNkK2ZZbXdkUF9PLmxFG1RR0iFpBsKVAKcsHl6FN9pbxfOHdLQNc4MM-2FbU4VWtghMIdNAg9EwAPxF3SKWYhFsr-2FCzCEQ8FTAY-2FmkRJZjbJNmL98XI6PQrkxJmT60OR1s7-2FyfyH5F-2Bi11UxqoCMKt2uTae6xkjuQ8SyzX-2F8YrHsFq9aC6ImQQ-3D-3D  in Dahlgren, Va.


Supervisory Program Analyst - https://u3445751.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.dRmKBBepCL5WdMYvln7I9edxa57dOCZ5faCLZWyLNFP4LLcsktu1xRHiEjBASigbDr_G_wC5ozFR3fdJxOIYV8k7pfvLhj7Y4IzIFYHx6APv06-2Fj5awj7y7UZ-2FYUfo7mxh7pjCz4ynHspPMyNhNqik3rYryKRlJN7mi8b61BBMwwB91Ui7yzAHJGlEOB6wzQ7-2FNeRp2-2BTaO1CCRzl327T4wL2Qg0qwA-2FSMYCBjdSU8ReNePx6kr-2BIOsbwgIZU-2B8NQDA-2FidvVaRH07DyDHCPLlId5miU7SkQ3CYaBnhQnNkK2ZZbXdkUF9PLmxFG1RR0iFpBsKqS-2Fv0VolJ2myW6ksghqPpgXICJQnMHtrRVjh8fUI5LGrpZfBEZRqmCclHupNJxzxky8DxuF4LB5V76vna2LSiZo1hN5QjVbyGeHytPQXZkE9IRlj09XsxEBL50kLLrPSSVHGWROABjZzGxlTk7-2F8tQ-3D-3D  in Norfolk, Va.

Rules, policies, and cancellations

Wed, Feb 25, 2026 - Sun, Mar 01, 2026

  • Seat assignments not available until after check-in
  • Refunds and ticket changes are not permitted
  • Same-day confirmed and same-day standby travel changes are not permitted
  • Priority Boarding not available for purchase
  • IMPORTANT - Limitation on Use of Flight Credits: If you use a flight credit from a waiver to book a trip through Chase Travel, any unused portion of the credit will be forfeited, notwithstanding any provisions to the contrary in the airline's contract of carriage or fare rules. No such forfeiture will occur if the credit is used for a flight of greater or equal value to the credit.
  • Carry-on baggage restrictions will apply. The selected airline's baggage fees will apply. Click here for details.
  • If this is an international flight, this reservation requires proof of citizenship. Please contact the consulate destination for current VISA/Passport entry requirements.
  • The Transportation Security Administration requires specific information for each passenger such as full name, date of birth and gender to be provided to the airline at least 72 hours prior to departure. If this information was not provided for each passenger at the time of booking, it is the passenger's responsibility to ensure this information is provided to the airline to prevent additional screening, travel delays or denied transport. For more information, refer to TSA's website at TSA.
  • Important Notice Regarding Airline Fees: Airlines may charge additional fees for miscellaneous services including baggage. Fees vary by airline so you must contact the airline directly or check their website for up-to-date information and pricing details.
  • Your personal data will be processed in accordance with the applicable carrier's privacy policy and, if your booking is made via a reservation system provider ("GDS"), with its privacy policy. These are available at https://www.iatatravelcentre.com/privacy.htm or from the carrier or GDS directly. You should read this documentation, which applies to your booking and specifies, for example, how your personal data is collected, stored, used, disclosed and transferred.
  • Please refer to the Terms and Conditions for more information.
  • Cancellations may incur a cancellation fee up to the full amount of the ticket.
  • Changes may incur a change fee up to the full amount of the ticket plus any difference in the applicable fare.
  • This ticket is non-refundable.
  • Some non-refundable tickets can be applied (for a limited time) toward future travel, upon the collection of a change fee up to the full amount of the ticket plus any difference in the applicable fare.
  • Changes to your itinerary are generally permitted upon the collection of a change fee up to the full amount of the ticket plus any difference in the applicable fare. Some tickets do not allow changes.

Questions?

Hi Lauren,

I hope you had a great weekend!

I am reaching out because my college recently announced that our commencement ceremony will take place on May 21st. Although I will officially complete my coursework next Fall, I had been planning to walk with my classmates this Spring, which now interferes with orientation for this summer's internship session.

I wanted to ask if it might be possible to complete the orientation requirements on a different day? I attended orientation last summer and had a wonderful experience, but it would mean a lot to me to be able to participate in commencement as well.

Please let me know if this would be possible, or if it would impact my start date or relocation. Thank you so much for your consideration!

Best,
Cristina Le

subject: Create Your CartusOnline Account
body:
Welcome to Cartus!
Caterpillar has partnered with Cartus to provide assistance during your transition to Chicago, Illinois.
Get Started

Dear Cristina Le,

As your Cartus representative, I look forward to working with you throughout your transition. The first step is to create an account for the CartusOnline website. CartusOnline will be your source for tools and information, and will guide you to important details that need your immediate attention.

I encourage you to create your account and begin the process today. The Get Started button in this email is unique to you and is part of our secure account creation process. Once you select the Get Started button, you will be guided through the steps to create your account.

The CartusOnline Home page is customized for you and will provide you with status and next steps each time you log in. The website also contains resources to help you during your transition, such as Area Information and Checklists. I am also available to assist if you have any questions.

Already have a CartusOnline Account? Look for the checkbox on the Verification Code page, and be prepared to enter your existing Cartus User ID and Password to link your accounts.

Get Started

Best wishes,

Carolina Yamada

Cartus
Tel: 55 11 2395 8170
Carolina.Yamada@cartus.com
www.cartus.com

For help, please contact

Cartus I.T. Help Desk

1-800-316-8070 (intra US/Canada)

+1 203-205-1726 (outside the US)

Please do not reply to this email notification. Thank you.

where mobility meets agility®

Visit us at www.cartus.com.
